Japanese: ２月　９日　午前　９時４７分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: February 9, 9:47 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: まあ‥‥マシスさまが、 私のことを‥‥？
English: Oh my... Mr. Laurice feels that way about... me...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥あなたの《秘密》を 知っていたのではないようです。
English: Apparently... he isn't aware of your real secret at all.

Japanese: ナニをテレているのですか！ こんなときに！
English: This is no time to be embarrassed!

Japanese: すす、すみません！
English: I-I'm sorry!

Japanese: 私‥‥そういうコトに 不慣れなものですから‥‥
English: I'm just... hardly accustomed to that sort of thing.

Japanese: まあ‥‥事件当夜、アイツが 何を見たのか‥‥
English: Worry not... And in any case, whatever it was that he saw on the night of the incident...

Japanese: この私が、かならず 引きずり出してみせましょう。
English: Mark my words, I will drag it out him!

Japanese: 今日の証人は‥‥ マシスさま、なのでしょうか？
English: Does that mean... Mr. Laurice is the witness today...?

Japanese: ‥‥いや。
まず出てくるのは‥‥ おそらく、あの住職です。
English: I believe that nun... will be the first to take the stand.

Japanese: 毘忌尼さま‥‥
English: Sister Bikini...

Japanese: 彼女は、あなたの犯行の瞬間を 目撃したと言っている。
English: She claims to have seen the very instant in which you carried out the crime.

Japanese: もう一度だけ、 カクニンしておきましょう。
English: I just want to ask you one last time.

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスを殺害したのは‥‥ あなたではないのですね？
English: It really wasn't you who killed Ms. Elise Deauxnim, correct?

Japanese: はい。私ではございません。
English: That is correct. It wasn't me.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば、よろしい。
English: ...Very well then.

Japanese: あの‥‥御剣さま？
English: Um... Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。
English: ...Yes?

Japanese: 御剣さまは‥‥検事さん、 なのですよね。
English: You are a prosecutor... aren't you?

Japanese: 大丈夫なのですか？　もし、 正体がバレてしまったら‥‥
English: Are you sure about this? If your true identity is revealed...

Japanese: シンパイは無用。 ‥‥手は打ってあります。
English: Don't worry. ...I've taken the necessary steps.

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか‥‥
English: Y-You have...?

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。
English: ...Iris.

Japanese: 検事のシゴトは‥‥ 人を疑うことです。
English: It is a prosecutor's job to doubt people.

Japanese: しかし‥‥。 今日の私は、弁護士だ。
English: But right now... I am a defense attorney.

Japanese: 弁護士のシゴトは、 人を信じ抜くこと‥‥
English: A defense attorney's job is to believe in people, and to believe until the bitter end.

Japanese: 私の友人は、そう言っていました。
English: That's what a friend of mine told me once.

Japanese: 御剣さま‥‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 私に、そのシゴトができるか どうか‥‥
English: You may pass judgment on me from the defendant's chair. You are the one to decide...

Japanese: 被告席で、シッカリ 見ていただきたい。
English: ...whether or not I am able to do the task I have been entrusted.

Japanese: ‥‥よろしく おねがいいたします。
English: ...Very well, sir. I leave my defense in your capable hands.

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第７法廷
English: February 9, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 7

Japanese: これより、葉桜院　あやめの 法廷を、しめやかに開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Sister Iris of Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備は完了している。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥察するところ‥‥
English: ...The defense does indeed appear to be ready...

Japanese: 検察側は、準備が できていないようですね。
English: However, the same cannot be said for the prosecution in this case.

Japanese: ‥‥そのようだ。
English: ...Indeed.

Japanese: 審理にムダな時間をかけるのは、 どうかと思いますな。
English: I'm not sure I like such blatant waste of this court's time.

Japanese: 検事席がカラ、ということは‥‥
English: An empty prosecutor's chair can only mean...

Japanese: 被告人の有罪を立証する 自信がない、ということです。
English: ...that the prosecutor has no confidence in their ability to prove their case.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、 勝負あったようですな。
English: It would seem this case is already over before it had a chance to begin.

Japanese: それでは、しめやかに 判決を言いわたしましょう！
English: I am ready to announce my verdict at this time. This court finds the defendant...

Japanese: ‥‥検察側。準備完了‥‥
English: The prosecution... stands ready.

Japanese: あ‥‥あなたは‥‥？
English: A-And you are...?

Japanese: 狩魔　冥（かるまめい）。 ‥‥天才検事よ。
English: Franziska von Karma... Prosecuting prodigy.

Japanese: か、かるま検事‥‥というと‥‥
English: V-Von Karma, you say...?

Japanese: あの伝説の検事、狩魔　豪 （かるまごう）検事の‥‥？
English: Perchance, you wouldn't be of any relation to the legendary prosecutor Manfred von Karma?

Japanese: 伝説は、すでに過去のもの。 この私が、狩魔。‥‥それだけよ。
English: Legends are a thing of the past. I am a Von Karma. That is all.

Japanese: 今回、特別に依頼を受けて アメリカから来たわ。
English: Upon a special request, I flew in today for the purposes of prosecuting this case.

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか！ それは、なんとも‥‥大物です！
English: Y-You did!? Then... you must be quite a big shot, eh!

Japanese: ‥‥ところで‥‥弁護人。
English: By the way, Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: なんだろうか。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: どうも‥‥あなたには、見おぼえが あるような気がするのですが。
English: I'm almost certain that I've seen you somewhere before. Or am I just imagining things?

Japanese: かつて、よく似た検事に 会ったことがあるような‥‥
English: You look very much like a prosecutor I met once...

Japanese: ‥‥気のせいだろう。
English: ...I believe you are imagining things, Your Honor.

Japanese: いかがかな？　狩魔検事。
English: Ms. von Karma? Do you have anything to say?

Japanese: 検事局に、このような ヤワなオトコは存在しない。
English: There is no such weakling as this man among those of the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: そ、そうでしたか？ しかし‥‥前に一度、この法廷で
English: There... there isn't...? But I'm sure... once before, in this courtroom...

Japanese: うはッ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ‥‥存在しない、と言っている。
English: ...I told you, there is no such weakling!

Japanese: な、なんですか！　そのムチは！ 私は、どうかと思いますぞッ！
English: Wh-What is that!? A whip!? I'm not sure I care for such a thing in my courtroom!

Japanese: か、係官！　今すぐ、 そのムチを‥‥
English: B-Bailiff! Remove that whip, at...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側に、異議なし。
English: ...I have no objection to the whip.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: Y-You don't...?

Japanese: 検察側がムチをふるおうと、 コーヒーをガブ飲みしようと‥‥
English: The prosecution can wield a whip or drink seventeen cups of coffee...

Japanese: ‥‥真実は、１つしかない。 私が立証してごらんに入れよう。
English: But there is still only one truth. That is what I stand here to prove today!

Japanese: おもしろいわ‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: This promises to be interesting... Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥私は当然、今日の相手は 成歩堂　龍一だと思っていた。
English: ...I had expected to face Phoenix Wright here today.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: But looking at you now...

Japanese: 本当は‥‥このときを 待っていたのかもしれない。
English: ...maybe this is what I have been waiting for all this time.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍！　‥‥あなたを 打ち負かす、このチャンスを！
English: Miles Edgeworth! I will not allow this chance to crush you slip through my fingers!

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: ...I see you brought your flair for the histrionic.

Japanese: こ‥‥‥この異様なフンイキ。 私はどうかと思いますな。
English: Allow me to add to the things I'm not sure about... People acting bizarrely in my court!

Japanese: うははッ！
English: Uwa-hah!

Japanese: ‥‥舞台はととのったわ。 審理を進めなさいな、裁判長。
English: ...The stage is set. Now continue with the proceedings, Your Honor.

Japanese: それでは‥‥狩魔検事。 事件の説明をおねがいします。
English: Very well. Ms. von Karma, please give an outline of this case.

Japanese: くれぐれも、しめやかに！
English: With as little whipping as possible!

Japanese: 殺害されたのは、絵本作家の 天流斎　エリス。
English: The murder victim is the famed picture book author, Ms. Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: ‥‥その死体は、葉桜院の 境内で発見されたわ。
English: Her body was found in the Hazakura Temple courtyard.

Japanese: 腹部を、黄金像の宝刀によって 刺しつらぬかれて、ね。
English: She had been stabbed through the torso by a ceremonial sword from a golden statue.

Japanese: これが‥‥その宝刀ですか‥‥
English: The sword in this picture... is the weapon in question, correct...?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 その現場写真を受理しましょう。
English: ...Very well. The court accepts this photo of the crime scene.

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 尼僧・あやめの犯行で あることは、まちがいないわ。
English: There is no mistake. This was the doing of Sister Iris.

Japanese: なにしろ‥‥ 犯行の目撃者がいるのだから。
English: After all... there is a witness to her crime.

Japanese: ‥‥了解しました。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: それでは、その証人を 入廷させてください！
English: Please bring this witness to the stand!

Japanese: （いよいよ、始まるな‥‥）
English: (And so it begins...)

Japanese: （弁護士として‥‥ 最初で、最後の法廷が！）
English: (My first and my last trial... as a defense attorney!)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。名前と職業を おねがいするわ。
English: ...Witness. State your name and occupation. Please.

Japanese: いやいや！　お待ちなさい！ 私は、どうかと思いますぞ！
English: H-Hold on here! I'm not sure about being not sure if I care for this at all!

Japanese: 証人！　ちょっと、しめやかに 背のびしてもらえますかな。
English: Witness! Please stand up nice and straight!

Japanese: たしか、控え室に‥‥
English: If I recall correctly...

Japanese: 背が不自由な証人のための みかん箱があったはずだ。
English: ...there are a few milk crates in the defendant's lobby for witnesses with bad backs.

Japanese: 係官！　ひとっ走り、 おねがいします！
English: Bailiff! Fetch a crate for this poor lady, please!

Japanese: ‥‥では、あらためて 名前と職業をおねがいするわ。
English: ...Once again. Your name and occupation, please.

Japanese: オバサンね。吾童山、葉桜院の 住職さん、やっておりますの。
English: Little old me? Well, I'm the head nun of Hazakura Temple on Eagle Mountain.

Japanese: 名前はね。ビキニよビキニ。 どうか、お見知りおきを。
English: My name is Bikini. You got it? Bikini. Nice to meet everyone.

Japanese: 見たところ、ビキニでは ないようですが‥‥？
English: But you don't appear to be wearing a bikini right now...

Japanese: うはははッ！
English: Uwah!

Japanese: ‥‥法廷は、神聖なる裁きの庭‥‥
English: ...The courtroom is the garden of holy judgment.

Japanese: いやしいココロの持ち主は、 ただちに出て行きなさいッ！
English: Those with lechery in their hearts should leave this sanctuary at once!

Japanese: わわ、私が出ていくんですか！
English: Y-You want me to leave!?

Japanese: まあまあ。オバサンだってねぇ、 じつはスゴいんだから。特に夏は。
English: No need to get your bikinis in a twist! Let me tell you, I'm a sight to behold in summer!

Japanese: わはは。わは。わははははわは
English: Wha ha ha! Ha ha ha! Ha ha!

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく‥‥
English: In any case...

Japanese: 証人。事件当夜、あなたは犯行の 瞬間を目撃した、と聞いているが。
English: Witness. I hear that you saw the crime take place on the night in question.

Japanese: そ、そおなのよねェ！
English: That's right!

Japanese: いやいや。オバサンもね。 まだ、自分が信じられないワケ。
English: I can still hardly believe it myself, to be honest.

Japanese: まさか‥‥ウチのあやめが あんなコト、するわけないし‥‥
English: There's no way dear little Iris could do anything like that...

Japanese: それでは‥‥、 お話を聞くとしましょうか。
English: Let us hear what you have to say then.

Japanese: まず、事件当夜の、 証人自身の行動について！
English: First, tell us aboot your own movements that night, eh!

Japanese: ～事件当夜のこと～
English: -- The Night of the Murder --

Japanese: あの晩、オバサンは《奥の院》で 修験者さまの修行を手伝ってたの。
English: That night I was helping an acolyte with her training in the Inner Temple. But...

Japanese: でも‥‥こう見えて、オバサン。 コシが悪いのよ。暴力的なまでに。
English: Well, as you can see, my back likes to act up. Violently.

Japanese: だからね。あやめにアトを任せて、 葉桜院に帰ってきたワケ。
English: So, I left Iris to help the acolyte, and returned to Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: 奥の院って、おフロがないから。 オバサン、ゆっくりあったまって。
English: There's no bath at the Inner Temple, you see, and I needed a long, hot soak.

Japanese: さて、戻ろうか‥‥ってときに！ みみ、見ちゃったのよ！
English: It was after I had finished, just as I was heading back... that's when I saw it!

Japanese: ぬううう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: それでは、証人が葉桜院に 戻ったのは、グーゼンだった‥‥？
English: So it was simply coincidence that you found yourself returning to Hazakura Temple?

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、そうだわね。
English: ...Yes, you could say that.

Japanese: コシが痛まなきゃ、ずっと 奥の院にいたと思いますよ。
English: If my back hadn't been in so much pain, I would have stayed at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: （それは‥‥ 重要かもしれないな‥‥）
English: (That sounds like a pretty important statement she just made...)

Japanese: この証言で、問題に なるのは‥‥ただ１点。
English: There is only one problem with this testimony that I can see...

Japanese: まあ‥‥こんなトコロで つまずくオトコじゃないわよね？
English: And you're not about to fall at the first hurdle, now are you, Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. ...Please begin your cross- examination.

Japanese: ～事件当夜のこと～
English: -- The Night of the Murder --

Japanese: あの晩、オバサンは《奥の院》で 修験者さまの修行を手伝ってたの。
English: That night I was helping an acolyte with her training in the Inner Temple. But...

Japanese: でも‥‥こう見えて、オバサン。 コシが悪いのよ。暴力的なまでに。
English: Well, as you can see, my back likes to act up. Violently.

Japanese: だからね。あやめにアトを任せて、 葉桜院に帰ってきたワケ。
English: So, I left Iris to help the acolyte, and returned to Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: 奥の院って、おフロがないから。 オバサン、ゆっくりあったまって。
English: There's no bath at the Inner Temple, you see, and I needed a long, hot soak.

Japanese: さて、戻ろうか‥‥ってときに！ みみ、見ちゃったのよ！
English: It was after I had finished, just as I was heading back... that's when I saw it!

Japanese: （この証言で、問題になるのは ‥‥たしかに、ただ１点のみ）
English: (There is indeed only one problem with this testimony.)

Japanese: （それをハッキリさせることで、 たしかめることができる）
English: (If I can clearly point out what it is, then I can begin to quantify...)

Japanese: （‥‥あの証人の 記憶力と、観察力を‥‥）
English: (...just how good this witness's memory and observation skills are.)

Japanese: あの子、ちゃんと奥の院に来たわ。 夕食のときと同じカッコウでね。
English: Iris came to the Inner Temple. She was dressed exactly as she had been at dinner.

Japanese: 《奥の院》というのは‥‥？
English: What is this Inner Temple...?

Japanese: オバサンたちはホラ。“霊行密道” の修行をするワケじゃない？
English: Well, see, conversing with the spirits is what we train people to do, right?

Japanese: ワレワレにカクニンを 求められても困ります！
English: We'll be the ones asking you the questions, madam.

Japanese: ‥‥そのためにはね。霊気の集う 修行場が必要なワケ。
English: ...In order to do that, a place strong in spiritual power is required.

Japanese: おぼろ橋をわたった先に、 《奥の院》という庵があって。
English: There's a small temple across Dusky Bridge, called the Inner Temple.

Japanese: 修験者さまは、そこで一晩中、 霊行をつむのよォ。
English: Acolytes must spend an entire night there to undergo intense training.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、あなたの 《手伝い》というのは‥‥？
English: And how exactly do you help with this process...?

Japanese: 霊行は、かなりの荒行だから。 住職がつきそうキマリなのね。
English: It is all quite exacting. It can't be performed without a nun supervising.

Japanese: デキの悪い小学生を見張る 家庭教師のようなモノね。
English: Like a tutor, watching to make sure a spoiled child studies.

Japanese: （‥‥その家庭教師は、きっと ムチを持っているのだろうな）
English: (A tutor with a whip, in your case.)

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。 それならば、なぜ‥‥
English: If that is the case...

Japanese: 事件が起こった葉桜院へと 戻って来たのだろうか？
English: ...then why did you return to Hazakura Temple, where the murder took place?

Japanese: 暴力的‥‥
English: Violently...?

Japanese: も。ホント、シャレにならないわ。 スゴいんだから。特に冬は。
English: That's right. It's no laughing matter, especially in winter!

Japanese: オバサン、冬はハシより重いもの、 持ったコトないもん。
English: I can't hold anything heavier than a knife and fork during the cold months!

Japanese: 生きてるだけで、これはもう 修行みたいなモンだわ。
English: Just being alive is like strict training!

Japanese: わはは。わは。わははははわは
English: Wah ha ha! Wha ha! Wah ha ha!

Japanese: ‥‥あの晩も、その ジマンのコシが‥‥？
English: On the night of the murder, was this fabulous back of yours hurting again...?

Japanese: そ。ガクガク暴れだしたワケ。 オバサン、失神しかけて。
English: That's right. Raging like a bull in a pigpen. I almost fainted once or twice!

Japanese: これはもう、あったまらないと コシに殺される、って思ったの。
English: I just knew that unless I warmed it up, it was going to finally finish me off!

Japanese: “アトを任せた”‥‥というのは？
English: You "left Iris to help"... With what?

Japanese: 修験者さまの修行に 決まってるでしょ。
English: What do you think? The acolyte's training, of course.

Japanese: １０時をすぎて、ちょうど 《本行》に入るところだったから。
English: It was just past 10 PM, so we were starting to enter into the training exercises proper.

Japanese: それは‥‥、 あなたの役目だったのでは？
English: Wasn't it your place to remain with the disciple?

Japanese: ま。要は修験者さまが 死なないように見守る係だから。
English: Well, the job is simply to watch over the acolyte so that they don't pass away.

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、カクニンする。 あなたは、あの晩‥‥
English: Just to confirm this point again, that night...

Japanese: 奥の院で、あやめさんに 会ったのですね？
English: ...you met Iris in the Inner Temple, correct?

Japanese: ええ。ええ。だって、あの子は スナオでいい子ですからね。
English: Yes, yes. She's a gentle, honest girl.

Japanese: 言いつけを守らなかったコト なんて、ないんですよォ。
English: She's never once failed to follow my directions.

Japanese: ‥‥入浴するために 葉桜院に戻った、と‥‥？
English: ...So you returned to the Hazakura Temple in order to take a bath?

Japanese: ぜぇんぶ、オバサンのコシが 悪いのよねェ。
English: My back is to blame for everything.

Japanese: もお、“やるかやられるか” の世界だから。こればっかりは。
English: It's a "do or be done in" kind of world, after all.

Japanese: それで‥‥入浴には、どれぐらいの 時間がかかったのだろうか？
English: How long were you in the bath for, if you don't mind me asking?

Japanese: あらあらあら。いやだョこの子は！ ヘンな想像しちゃって！
English: My my my, what a filthy little rogue you are! I know what's on your mind!

Japanese: 次に“どこから洗うのだろうか？” とか、聞くつもりなんでしょ！
English: I bet your next question is going to be, "Where exactly did you wash?"

Japanese: ‥‥まったく、ワカい男の子は コレだからねェ‥‥
English: Ah, THIS is why you have to watch the young ones...

Japanese: なな、何を言う！　私は‥‥
English: W-What are you going on about!? I was...

Japanese: ぐはァッ！
English: Gwah!

Japanese: ‥‥フケツよ、御剣　怜侍。
English: ...Pathetic, Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥最低ですな。
English: The lowest of the low.

Japanese: （な、なんだ、ここは！ いじめられっ子の席か‥‥？）
English: (Is there some sort of "Kick Me" sign stuck to the defense's bench!?)

Japanese: ま。オバサンも、あまり長いこと 席をはずしちゃマズいから‥‥
English: Anyway, I couldn't afford to be away from my post for too long, you understand, so...

Japanese: ‥‥事件は、境内で起こった わけだが‥‥？
English: The crime took place in the courtyard, correct...?

Japanese: オバサンの部屋から本堂に 行こうとすると‥‥、
English: When you go from my room to the Main Hall...

Japanese: かならず、境内を見わたす わたりろうかを通るのよねェ。
English: ...you have to take a winding hallway from which you can see the courtyard.

Japanese: そう！　つまり‥‥
English: That's right. In other words...

Japanese: 証人が事件を目撃したのは、 グーゼンだった‥‥
English: ...it was pure coincidence that the witness saw the crime taking place before her eyes.

Japanese: この事件に、いっさいの 作為は存在しなかったの。
English: There was no complicated set-up in this case!

Japanese: ぬうう‥‥ どうやら、そのようですな。
English: Hmm... That certainly seems to be true.

Japanese: 記憶ちがい、ということは ないだろうか？
English: Are you sure that you're not making a mistake...?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタねえ。あんまり オバサンをナメるんじゃないよ！
English: ...You, young man, need to get your estimation of me up from the floor!

Japanese: うム‥‥ッ！
English: Nngh...!

Japanese: あやめはいつも、 同じ装束を着ているんだよ。
English: Iris always wears the same clothes.

Japanese: 少しでもちがったら、 逆に印象に残るじゃないの！
English: The smallest thing out of place would have stood out like a sore thumb to me!

Japanese: 《記憶ちがい》ィ‥‥？ アンタのほうが、オカドちがいさ！
English: You're making a mistake, thinking I made a mistake!

Japanese: ‥‥うまくまとめましたな、証人。
English: ...An excellent finish there, witness.

Japanese: （はたして、そうだろうか‥‥）
English: (Still, I have to wonder...)

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、つねに試されている。
English: ...Witnesses have to undergo their own trials, I'm afraid.

Japanese: その観察力と、記憶力‥‥それが、 被告人の運命を決めるからです。
English: The defendant's fate rests on their powers of observation and memory, after all.

Japanese: まあまあ。まあまあまあ。 オバサンならね。大丈夫よォ！
English: Well, well, well. Don't worry! I'm more than up to the task!

Japanese: なんてったってホラ。住職だから。 エラいから。葉桜院じゃイチバン。
English: I'm a woman of faith, after all. The head honcho of Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば‥‥この証言を 説明してもらえるだろうか。
English: In that case, Ms. Honcho... I'd like you to explain something for me.

Japanese: 被告人・あやめさんの証言です。
English: The discrepancy between your testimony and that of the defendant, Iris.

Japanese: 《事件当夜は、鐘を鳴らしたあと、 自室にいた》‥‥
English: She claims that after ringing the lights out bell, she went back and stayed in her room.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！　彼女は、 奥の院に行っていないのです！
English: ...Which means! She did not go to the Inner Temple at all!

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥！ あの子が、そんなコトを‥‥ N... No...!
English: She said that...?

Japanese: “被告人”と、“証人”‥‥
English: A defendant or a witness...

Japanese: ウソをつく確率が高いのは どっちかしら？
English: Who is more likely to lie, do you suppose?

Japanese: 被告人が、自分の身を守るため、 そんなウソをついたのよ！
English: The defendant is simply lying to cover her back!

Japanese: それでは、スジがとおらない。
English: But that is completely illogical.

Japanese: 殺人現場は、葉桜院の境内だ。 被告人が、身を守るつもりなら‥‥
English: The murder was committed in the courtyard of Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: 《事件当夜は、奥の院に行った》 ‥‥そう、証言するハズなのだ。
English: Claiming that she "went to the Inner Temple" would make for a much better alibi.

Japanese: でも‥‥オカシイわ。
English: But that is odd...

Japanese: どんな理由であれ‥‥ あの子がウソをつく、なんて‥‥
English: Whatever the reason... I can't believe that she would lie...!

Japanese: ぬうう‥‥たしかに、 スナオそうな目をしています。
English: Hmm... she does indeed have honest eyes!

Japanese: うははははッ！
English: Uwaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥どんな人間も、ウソをつく。 私の持論よ！
English: ...All people lie. That is my belief.

Japanese: ‥‥どうして私ばっかり たたくのですか‥‥
English: Why am I the only one being whipped in here...!?

Japanese: とにかく！　被告人、証人とも ウソをつく理由はありません。
English: Anyway! Neither the witness nor the defendant have any reason to lie!

Japanese: ‥‥と、なると‥‥
English: Which means...

Japanese: あなたの記憶力が問題に なりますね‥‥
English: We must call your memory into question...

Japanese: おやおや。オバサンより年上の クセに、記憶力にケチつける気？
English: Dear, dear, dear. You're older than me and yet you want to play that game, do you?

Japanese: そそ、そういうワケでは‥‥
English: A-Ah, well, that isn't exactly what I...

Japanese: オバサンの記憶力はねェ！ すごいんだから！　特に冬は。
English: My memory is perfect! Crystal clear! Especially in winter!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥尋問を終了するのは まだ、早いようです。
English: Then... I suppose it's too early to end this cross- examination, eh.

Japanese: 弁護人。証人の記憶力を 問題にするのであれば‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth. If you are going to question the memory of this witness...

Japanese: もっと、決定的な証拠を 見せていただきませんとね。
English: ...you will need to show me a more decisive piece of evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥了解した。 （まだ、アマかったようだ‥‥）
English: ...Understood, Your Honor. (I was naïve to think that alone would do the trick.)

Japanese: それでは。あやめさんのことを 証言に加えていただいて‥‥
English: Then please add your comments aboot Iris to the testimony...

Japanese: 尋問に戻ります。 ‥‥よろしいですな？
English: And let us return to the cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。１つだけ、 たしかめておこう。
English: ...Witness. Let's get one thing straight.

Japanese: あなたが会ったと主張する “被告人”は‥‥
English: The defendant whom you claim to have met...

Japanese: この《退魔のずきん》を かぶっていただろうか？
English: ...she was wearing this "Demon-Warding Hood", correct?

Japanese: もちろんよォ！
English: Of course!

Japanese: オバサンたちにとって、とっても ダイジなものですからねェ。
English: That is a very important piece of clothing, I'll have you know!

Japanese: わはは。わは。わはははは‥‥ あれ？
English: Wha ha ha! Wha ha ha! Ha ha... Wait a minute...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ちなさい！ どうしてあなたが、それを‥‥？
English: H-Hold it right there! Why do you have that...!?

Japanese: それが、問題なのだよ。 ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: That's the question of the day, now isn't it... Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: このずきんは、事件当夜。 ある人物にプレゼントされた。
English: I'll have you know that this hood was given to someone as a gift that night.

Japanese: ‥‥《消灯の鐘》を鳴らす前に。
English: Before the lights out bell was rung.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: W... What!?

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに もう、おわかりだろう。
English: ...You know where I'm going with this, don't you?

Japanese: 事件当夜、証人が 被告人を見たとしたら‥‥
English: If the witness had seen the defendant as she claims...

Japanese: ずきんをかぶっている はずがないのだッ！
English: ...the defendant couldn't have been wearing this very hood!

Japanese: まあまあまあまあ！
English: Well well well!

Japanese: （ムジュンを暴く、この感覚 ‥‥なかなか、悪くない）
English: (It's not a bad feeling at all, exposing contradictions like this...)

Japanese: （成歩堂のうれしそうな カオも、ナットクできる‥‥）
English: (Now I understand that happy look on Wright's face every time he does it...)

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥いかがですか、証人！
English: Order! Order in the court! Witness, your response...

Japanese: うはははははッ！
English: Wuahaaa!

Japanese: ‥‥ジューショクさま。
English: ...Sister.

Japanese: そのずきん‥‥もちろん、 予備があるはずよね。
English: This hood... You have spare ones around the temple, don't you?

Japanese: “予備”‥‥というか‥‥
English: Spares... Well...

Japanese: いくつか、よけいに 作ってありますけどねェ。
English: I do tend to make too many of them.

Japanese: ほほお‥‥。 あまっていたわけですか‥‥
English: I see... A stockpile. A surplus of hoods, eh?

Japanese: でも、僧に与えられるずきんは ヒトリに１つ、なんですよ。
English: Each nun is only given one hood.

Japanese: あやめの持っているずきんは、 それだけのハズ‥‥
English: This should be the only hood that Iris owned...

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥ それは、たしかに奇妙ですなあ。
English: Hmm... Then this is quite strange!

Japanese: うははははははッ！
English: Waaaah!

Japanese: ずきんがあまっていた以上‥‥ それをつけることは、可能。
English: If there was a surplus of hoods, then she could have worn one of those!

Japanese: ‥‥なんのムジュンも、ないッ！
English: ...There is no contradiction here!

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （ザンネンながら、狩魔検事‥‥ そうは行かないのだよ）
English: (I'm sorry to break this to you, Ms. von Karma... but you won't get away that easily.)

Japanese: （食いちがいは、人のココロに タネをまくのだ）
English: (Discrepancies such as this will sow seeds in any human heart.)

Japanese: （“疑惑”という名のタネを‥‥）
English: (The seeds of doubt.)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: ...Witness.

Japanese: あなたの発言を疑うつもりは ありませんが‥‥
English: While I don't wish to call your testimony into doubt...

Japanese: 証言には、細心の注意を 払っていただきたいですな。
English: ...you must give every detail with precision.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんか ナットク行かないけどねェ‥‥。
English: I-I'm not sure I'm comfortable going along with this...

Japanese: それでは、ジューショクさま。 つづけて証言をしていただくわ。
English: Sister. You shall continue with your testimony.

Japanese: 入浴後、あなたが奥の院に戻る際に 目撃したことを！
English: Tell us what you saw after finishing your bath, on your way back to the Inner Temple!

Japanese: （すでに“疑惑のタネ”は、 裁判長のココロで芽ぶいている）
English: (Those seeds of doubt are sprouting in the Judge's heart.)

Japanese: （それが実をむすぶには‥‥ もう少しだけ、シゲキが必要だ）
English: (They just need a little more stimulation to bear fruit...)

Japanese: （“ムジュン”という名の シゲキが‥‥な）
English: (Contradictory stimulation.)

Japanese: ～入浴後、目撃したこと～
English: -- After My Bath --

Japanese: おフロから出て、１１時ごろ‥‥ 奥の院に戻ろうと思ったワケ。
English: I finished my bath around 11, and I thought I should return to the Inner Temple.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥境内のほうから 物音が聞こえて。ふと見ると‥‥
English: And as I was walking back... I heard a noise from the court- yard. I took a look and...

Japanese: あ‥‥あやめが！　え、エリスさま を‥‥あろうことか、カタナでッ！
English: Iris was...! Oh, Mystic Elise! And with that sword, of all things!

Japanese: エリスさまは、境内に面した カド部屋にいらっしゃいました。
English: Mystic Elise was staying in the corner room, which faces out onto the courtyard.

Japanese: そこからつき落とされたあと、 カタナで刺されたんでしょうねえ。
English: The stabbing I saw must have occurred after she was pushed out of her window.

Japanese: それは‥‥タイヘンな光景を 目撃されましたね‥‥。
English: You saw a truly terrible sight, didn't you...?

Japanese: ‥‥たとえば、それを 私自身に置きかえてみれば‥‥
English: If I was in your place...

Japanese: 狩魔検事、なんと法廷にて 御剣弁護士をムチでまっぷたつッ！
English: ...then it would be much like Ms. von Karma whipping Mr. Edgeworth in two in court!

Japanese: 私、なんとそれを 裁判長席にて目撃ィッ！
English: And me, seeing it all from this very chair!

Japanese: ‥‥みたいなものですからねえ。
English: ...Er, well, something like that.

Japanese: （あの裁判長‥‥）
English: (This judge...)

Japanese: （イトノコギリ刑事と同レベルの 想像力を持っているようだ‥‥）
English: (His imagination is about as vivid and creative as Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: バカの想像力はバカなるがゆえ バカにするべきにあらず‥‥
English: I would look the fool if I commented on such foolishness.

Japanese: どうやら、勝負あったようね。 ‥‥御剣　怜侍！
English: Anyway, this case is mine... Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: その、ヒトの名をフルネームで呼ぶ クセ‥‥なんとかならないのか？
English: Calling everyone by their full name... Can't you do something about that habit of yours?

Japanese: ～入浴後、目撃したこと～
English: -- After My Bath --

Japanese: おフロから出て、１１時ごろ‥‥ 奥の院に戻ろうと思ったワケ。
English: I finished my bath around 11, and I thought I should return to the Inner Temple.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥境内のほうから 物音が聞こえて。ふと見ると‥‥
English: And as I was walking back... I heard a noise from the court- yard. I took a look and...

Japanese: あ‥‥あやめが！　え、エリスさま を‥‥あろうことか、カタナでッ！
English: Iris was...! Oh, Mystic Elise! And with that sword, of all things!

Japanese: エリスさまは、境内に面した カド部屋にいらっしゃいました。
English: Mystic Elise was staying in the corner room, which faces out onto the courtyard.

Japanese: そこからつき落とされたあと、 カタナで刺されたんでしょうねえ。
English: The stabbing I saw must have occurred after she was pushed out of her window.

Japanese: （‥‥ウソをついている ようすは感じられない）
English: (...It doesn't feel like she is lying.)

Japanese: （その証人が、ハッキリ 証言している‥‥）
English: (This is very powerful testimony, too.)

Japanese: （“被告人が、被害者をカタナで 刺す瞬間を見た”‥‥と）
English: (She claims to have seen the instant in which the defendant stabbed the victim...)

Japanese: （‥‥今の私が信じられるのは、 ２つだけ‥‥）
English: (There are only two things I can believe in right now...)

Japanese: （依頼人・あやめさんと‥‥ 私自身の、弁護士としての能力）
English: (My client, Iris, and my own abilities as a defense attorney.)

Japanese: そういえば‥‥あのときのあやめ、 ずきんをかぶってなかったわ‥‥
English: Now that you mention it... Iris didn't have her hood on.

Japanese: ‥‥奥の院までは、どれぐらいの キョリがあるのだろうか？
English: ...How far is it from your room to the Inner Temple?

Japanese: そうねえ‥‥。
English: Let me think a moment...

Japanese: オバサンの足だと、歩いて ２０分ぐらい、かしらねぇ。
English: About twenty minutes, on these stumps of mine.

Japanese: 葉桜院からおぼろ橋まで、 １５分ぐらいかかるのよォ。
English: It's about 15 minutes to Dusky Bridge from Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: 橋から奥の院までは、 すぐなんだけど。
English: The Inner Temple is just beyond the bridge.

Japanese: まあ‥‥ケッキョク、あの晩は そこへ帰ることはなかったわけね。
English: Still, you never made it back there that night, did you?

Japanese: そ、そうよねェ‥‥。
English: T-That's right...

Japanese: あれは、ろうかから本堂に 向かう途中だったんだけど‥‥
English: I was heading along the walkway toward the Main Hall...

Japanese: “物音”‥‥というと？
English: You say you heard a noise...?

Japanese: “ドサッ”‥‥みたいな。
English: "Thump"... Just like that.

Japanese: ‥‥被害者が倒れた音としか 考えられないわ。
English: ...That could only be the sound of the victim falling.

Japanese: なんといっても 静かだからねェ、ウチは。
English: It's very quiet in the temple, you know.

Japanese: 木の枝から雪が落ちる音も ハッキリ聞こえますよォ。
English: You can even hear the snow falling from the branches.

Japanese: “ドサッ”‥‥て。
English: "Thump"... Just like that.

Japanese: では‥‥その音も、雪が 落ちたときのものだったのでは？
English: But then, couldn't this noise you heard have been snow falling to the ground?

Japanese: それは思いつかなかったわ。
English: I never thought of that.

Japanese: わはは。わは。わはははは‥‥
English: Wah ha ha. Wha ha ha. Ha ha...

Japanese: ほほっ。ほほ。ほほほほほ‥‥
English: Hoh hoh. Hoh hoh. Hoh hoh...

Japanese: うはははははははッ！
English: Uaaaaaah!

Japanese: 出るクイは打たれる。 ‥‥ムチで。
English: The next one to laugh gets a whipping!

Japanese: ま。キッカケはどうあれ、 境内を見ると‥‥
English: Well. Whatever the source of the sound, I looked over at the courtyard and...

Japanese: ‥‥先ほども、証人は “被告人を見た”と証言した。
English: ...This is the second time that the witness has testified to seeing the defendant.

Japanese: しかし‥‥その発言には、 ギモンの余地が残されている。
English: But... some doubt remains in these claims.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだい、 その言いかたはッ！
English: Hey, just what does that mean!?

Japanese: ちょっとイイ男だからって、 チョーシに乗ってると‥‥
English: Just because you're a good- looking young man doesn't give you the right to...

Japanese: 被害者をカタナで刺した、 《ハンニン》のこと‥‥
English: The murderer who stabbed the victim with the sword...

Japanese: もう一度、よく思い出して いただきたい！
English: Sister Bikini, try to recall exactly who it was you saw! As clearly as you can!

Japanese: ううう‥‥まあ。ちょっと イイ男だから、ユルしちゃうけど。
English: Hmm... Well. You're a handsome young man, so I'll forgive you.

Japanese: あ！　そういえば‥‥
English: Oh! Now that you mention it...

Japanese: よく考えてみると、ひとつだけ‥‥ おかしなコトがあったわ。
English: There was something awfully strange about her...

Japanese: そおよ！　なんか、ずうっと ココロに引っかかってたんだけど！
English: Something that has been bugging me all this time!

Japanese: ‥‥話していただこう。
English: ...Please, don't keep us in suspense.

Japanese: ずきんなのよ、ずきん！
English: Her hood!
Her hood...?

Japanese: そおなのよ！ ‥‥思い出してきたわ‥‥
English: That's right! ...It's coming back to me...

Japanese: あのときのあやめは‥‥ ずきんをかぶってなかったのよ！
English: Iris... She wasn't wearing her hood!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか不自然だと 思ってたんですけどねェ。
English: I thought something was out of place, but...

Japanese: でも。今となっては、 アタリマエだったわ、こりゃ。
English: It all makes sense now, doesn't it?

Japanese: だって‥‥そのずきん、あやめは ヒトにあげちゃってたんでしょ？
English: After all, she'd given that hood away to someone, right?
...Urk!
Hah...

Japanese: 墓穴をほったようね、 御剣　怜侍‥‥
English: You've dug your own grave, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: いかがですかな？　弁護人。 今の証言は‥‥
English: What do you say, Mr. Edgeworth? Is this testimony important?

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、この方向は キズを広げるだけ、のようだ）
English: (...This line of questioning is only going to tear the wound open further.)

Japanese: ただ今の証人の発言は、 ある刑事の捜査ほどにイミなし。
English: These statements are as useless as the investigations of a certain detective.

Japanese: ‥‥記録の必要はあるまい。
English: ...There is no need to record them.

Japanese: ぬううううむ‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥それでは、 証言をつづけていただきましょう。
English: Very well. ...Please, continue with your testimony.
Hmph...

Japanese: （一見、こちらに不利な 証言だが‥‥）
English: (This may initially appear to put me at a disadvantage...)

Japanese: （今の私には、他に 手がかりが見えていない‥‥）
English: (But, I can't see any other leads at the moment...)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。ただ今の発言を、 証言に加えていただきたい。
English: ...Your Honor. I would like these new statements to be added to the testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: Heh... Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 自分のクビをしめるつもりなら、 このムチをお貸しするわ。
English: If you want to hang yourself, you need only to ask. I'll gladly lend you my whip!

Japanese: それでは、証人。証言に 追加をおねがいしましょう。
English: Witness. Add that statement to your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥はいはい。
English: ...No problem.

Japanese: 境内から見上げたところに ある部屋‥‥ですね？
English: The room the victim was staying in overlooked the courtyard, correct?

Japanese: ということは‥‥被害者の部屋は ２階にあったワケですかな？
English: Which means... the victim's room was on the second floor?

Japanese: いえいえ。葉桜院は、１階だて。 いわゆる、平屋よォ。
English: No, no. Hazakura Temple is a single story building.

Japanese: ただねぇ。山の急斜面に あるモンだから。
English: But the mountain itself slopes downward...

Japanese: 山門側と逆にある 客間のあたりは、どうしてもね。
English: ...which elevates the Main Gate side of the temple and the guest rooms in the back...

Japanese: ‥‥２階ぐらいの 高さになっちゃうワケ。
English: ...to about the height of a two story building.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。被害者は、 その部屋にいたのですな？
English: I see... And the victim was staying in one of these elevated rooms, correct?

Japanese: ええ‥‥あの方のニモツを 運んだの、オバサンですから‥‥
English: Yes... I should know. I'm the one who carried her things to her room, after all...

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、そんなことが あなたにわかるのだろうか？
English: What makes you so sure of all this...?

Japanese: だってホラ。さっき、オバサン 言ったでしょうが。
English: It's just like I told you earlier...

Japanese: “境内のほうで物音が聞こえた” ‥‥って。
English: "I heard a noise from the courtyard." OK?

Japanese: “ドサッ”‥‥みたいな。
English: "Thump"... Just like that.

Japanese: さすが、ジューショクさま。 なかなか、スルドいわね。
English: You're one smart sister, I'll give you that.

Japanese: 解剖記録にも書かれている とおり‥‥
English: The autopsy report states that...

Japanese: 被害者の全身には、打ち身の アトが残っていたわ。
English: ...the victim's body was covered in bruises...

Japanese: ‥‥３メートルほどの高さから 落下したていどの、ね。
English: ...indicating a fall from around 10 feet in height.

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥どうやら、 まちがいないようですな。
English: Hmm... It appears that the witness was not mistaken then.

Japanese: ね。ね。オバサン。 意外とヤルのよ。‥‥特に冬は。
English: Yep. Yep. I'm more than just a pretty face. Especially in winter!

Japanese: なんてったってホラ。住職だから。 エラいから。葉桜院じゃイチバン。
English: I'm a woman of faith, after all. The head honcho of Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: （あやめさんと２人しか いないではないか‥‥）
English: (There's only two of them working there...!)

Japanese: いかがかしら？　御剣　怜侍。 さすがに、コトバもないようね‥‥
English: What's wrong, Miles Edgeworth? No snappy comeback remark?

Japanese: それは‥‥まちがいないだろうか？
English: You are sure about that...?

Japanese: そうねぇ。オバサンたちってホラ。 いつも同じカッコ、してるから。
English: Yes. After all, we always wear the same clothes.

Japanese: あ。お金がないワケじゃないのよ！ スタイルだから。そういう。
English: Ah! I don't mean because we're poor, you understand? It's our style. Yes, that's it!

Japanese: ‥‥ベツに、あたふたと説明して もらわなくてもケッコウ、だ。
English: ...There's absolutely no need to explain yourself.

Japanese: とにかく！　いつもとちがうと、 スゴく目立つのよねェ。
English: Anyway! She looked different from normal, so that really stuck out.

Japanese: 私が、ムチのかわりに モチを持っているようなものね‥‥
English: Like me holding a whippet puppy instead of my whip.

Japanese: （そちらのほうが 圧倒的に平和だが‥‥）
English: (At least then it might bite you and not someone else...)

Japanese: あやめは、ずきんを つけてなかった。まちがいないね！
English: Iris didn't have her hood on. I'm sure of it!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: それでは、被害者のことを 聞かせていただきましょうか。
English: Now, please tell us aboot the victim, eh.

Japanese: ‥‥なかなかの名スイリ、 と言いたいところだが‥‥
English: ...Impressive logic. That's what I'd like to say, anyway.

Japanese: でしょお？　オバサンの頭脳、 スゴいんだから。‥‥特に冬は。
English: Oh please do! My brain is something else... especially in winter!

Japanese: ただ‥‥ひとつだけ、 巨大な見落としをしているようだ。
English: However... I think you are overlooking one thing.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。解剖記録を もう一度、よく見てほしい。
English: ...Ms. von Karma. Would you be so kind as to take another look at the autopsy report?

Japanese: か、解剖記録‥‥？
English: T-The autopsy report...?

Japanese: 被害者は、たしかに３メートルの 高さから、落下している。
English: The victim did fall from a height of 10 feet.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。
English: ...However.

Japanese: その《落下》は‥‥ 彼女の《死後》のことなのだ。
English: This fall... was after she was killed.

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！　ハッキリ 《死後》と書かれています！
English: T-That's right! It says "after death" right here!

Japanese: この証人が目撃した光景は、 アキラカにムジュンしている！
English: The scene the witness claims to have seen is contradictory!

Japanese: もし、あの境内で、被告人が 被害者を刺して殺害したのならば！
English: If the defendant stabbed and killed the victim there in the courtyard...

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、そこからどうやって ３メートルも落下できたのかッ！
English: ...how did the victim then go on to take a ten foot fall!?

Japanese: うううううッ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-Order! Order!

Japanese: 被害者は‥‥ 刺殺されたのち、落下した！
English: The victim was killed and then fell!

Japanese: そうなると‥‥
English: If that is the case...

Japanese: 本来の殺害現場は、被害者の部屋 ‥‥ということになるのですか？
English: ...then the victim must have been killed in her room... Don't you agree?

Japanese: そ‥‥、そうなるわね‥‥
English: Th-That is the logical conclusion...

Japanese: そうよ！　被害者は、自分の 部屋で、被告人に刺された！
English: Yes, that's right! The victim must have been stabbed by the defendant in her own room!

Japanese: そして、部屋の窓から 境内につき落とされた！
English: And she was then thrown out of her window down into the courtyard below!

Japanese: 天流斎エリスの部屋に、 犯行の痕跡はあったのかな‥‥？
English: Were there any signs of a struggle in Ms. Deauxnim's room...?

Japanese: カタナでつらぬかれたのだ。 かなりの出血があったはずだ。
English: She was stabbed with a sword! That would leave a blood stain, wouldn't you agree?

Japanese: い‥‥いかがですか、狩魔検事！ 出血のほうは‥‥
English: W-Well, Ms. von Karma? Was there any blood...?

Japanese: うははははははははッ！
English: Waaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥被害者の部屋から、 出血のアトは発見されなかった。
English: ...No traces of blood were found in the victim's room.

Japanese: 今のムチで、私のカオには ちょっと、血がニジみました。
English: ...Your whip has just caused traces of blood to be found on my glorious playoff beard.

Japanese: しかし！　部屋に血痕が なかった以上、検察側の主張は‥‥
English: However! If there was no blood in the room, then your claim that...

Japanese: うはははははははははッ！
English: Waaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。モチロン、 知ってるわよね‥‥？
English: I'm sure there's no need for me to go over this...

Japanese: 刃物による刺し傷の場合‥‥
English: ...as I'm sure Your Honor is well aware...

Japanese: 最もはげしく出血するのは、 どのタイミングか‥‥？
English: ...of when a stab wound produces the most blood.

Japanese: た、タイミング‥‥ですか？
English: When it produces the most blood...?

Japanese: 刺された瞬間の出血量‥‥ これは、たいしたことはない。
English: Very little blood is actually lost at the moment of a blade's insertion.

Japanese: 傷口から、もっとも 出血がはげしいのは‥‥
English: If you want to talk about when the most blood would be lost from a body...

Japanese: ‥‥凶器の刃物が、 傷口から引き抜かれたときよ。
English: ...that would be when the blade is removed.

Japanese: いかにも。 刃物が刺さっている状況では‥‥
English: Indeed. With the weapon still in place...

Japanese: いわば、その凶器自体が、 傷口の《フタ》となっているのだ。
English: ...it acts like a lid on the wound.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥刃物が刺さっている 状態では、出血は少ない‥‥
English: That's true... With the weapon still in the body, there wouldn't be much bleeding...

Japanese: そう考えられます！
English: A perfectly reasonable line of thinking!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥結論が出たようね！
English: We have come to a conclusion then!

Japanese: 被害者は、カタナで刺されたまま、 部屋からつき落とされた‥‥
English: The victim was thrown out of the window with the sword still in place...

Japanese: そう考えれば、 なんのムジュンもないッ！
English: This removes all of the contradictions!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 検事の主張には、可能性がある！
English: Order! Order! Order! I must admit that this is a probable version of events!

Japanese: （さすがは、狩魔　冥‥‥）
English: (I'd expect no less from Franziska von Karma...)

Japanese: （“ここしかない”という ポイントを押さえて来る‥‥！）
English: (She locates and takes control of every vital point!)
...Hmph...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: It seems...

Japanese: 証人に、もっとハッキリした 証言を要求しなければなりません。
English: ...that we need a clearer testimony from the witness.

Japanese: 勝手なスイソクはひかえて、 ジジツだけを述べてください！
English: Remove all supposition on your part and tell us only the truth, please!

Japanese: コラッ、証人！ みかん箱から下りないように！
English: Witness! Please! Remain standing on the crate!

Japanese: オバサンをナメるんじゃないよ。
English: Don't go selling me short now!

Japanese: 雪の重みで、冬のオバサンは 曲がり果ててるんだからね！
English: The weight of winter snow has bent me out of shape!

Japanese: ‥‥特に、コシと根性まわりが！
English: ...Especially my back and my mood!

Japanese: ジューショクさま。 証言をおねがいするわ。
English: Sister. Please give us your testimony.

Japanese: あとで、この私が カタとコシをたたいてあげるから。
English: I will give you a vigorous massage once we are finished here.

Japanese: （‥‥ムチでか？）
English: (...With the whip?)

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。 しょうがないねェ‥‥。
English: ...Oh, boy. Alright, alright...

Japanese: ～さらに詳しい状況～
English: -- Further Details --

Japanese: オバサンが見たとき、カタナは もう、刺さっていましたねェ。
English: When I looked across at the scene, the sword was already in place.

Japanese: 考えてみれば、あの子が刺した 瞬間は、見ていないみたい。
English: Thinking about it now, I didn't actually see her stab Mystic Elise.

Japanese: オバサン、あんなに血ィが出るのを 見たの、初めてだから‥‥
English: I've never seen so much blood before...

Japanese: それで、キゼツしちゃった のよねェ。ムリもないけど。
English: That's when I fainted. You can't blame me, can you?

Japanese: それで、目がさめたら‥‥ 供子さまが、エリスさまを‥‥！
English: And when I awoke... Mystic Ami was... stabbing Mystic Elise through the back!

Japanese: ぬううううむ‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥狩魔検事の主張が ウラづけられたようですな‥‥。
English: This all confirms Ms. von Karma's theory...

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔は、カンペキをもって よしとする‥‥
English: Von Karmas strive for nothing but perfection.

Japanese: このテイドは、当然のレベル。 そうでしょう？　御剣　怜侍‥‥
English: Putting together such facts is nothing for me! You should know that, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: （“カンペキ”など、 あり得ないのだ、狩魔　冥‥‥）
English: ("Perfection" is an impossibility, Franziska von Karma...)

Japanese: （どうやら、まだ わかっていないようだな‥‥）
English: (And I'm here to teach you just that...)

Japanese: ～さらに詳しい状況～
English: -- Further Details --

Japanese: オバサンが見たとき、カタナは もう、刺さっていましたねェ。
English: When I looked across at the scene, the sword was already in place.

Japanese: 考えてみれば、あの子が刺した 瞬間は、見ていないみたい。
English: Thinking about it now, I didn't actually see her stab Mystic Elise.

Japanese: オバサン、あんなに血ィが出るのを 見たの、初めてだから‥‥
English: I've never seen so much blood before...

Japanese: それで、キゼツしちゃった のよねェ。ムリもないけど。
English: That's when I fainted. You can't blame me, can you?

Japanese: それで、目がさめたら‥‥ 供子さまが、エリスさまを‥‥！
English: And when I awoke... Mystic Ami was... stabbing Mystic Elise through the back!

Japanese: （どうも、この事件は、 《不自然》が多すぎる‥‥）
English: (There are too many unnatural elements in this case...)

Japanese: （なぜ、供子像の七支刀を 凶器にする必要があったのか？）
English: (Why was it necessary to use the Shichishito from the Ami statue as a weapon?)

Japanese: （なぜ、その凶器を黄金像に 持たせる必要があったのか？）
English: (Why was the weapon finally placed back in the hand of the statue?)

Japanese: （この証人の証言の“不自然”を 暴けば、わかるのだろうか‥‥）
English: (If I can expose the flaws in this testimony, perhaps then I will begin to find the truth.)

Japanese: 根本まで刺さった凶器を抜く瞬間は 見たの。するする、スムーズに‥‥
English: I saw the instant in which the blade, plunged in to the hilt, was smoothly drawn out.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥被害者は 出血していただろうか？
English: At that time... was the victim bleeding?

Japanese: そうねえ‥‥オバサン、 それはもうビックリしたから。
English: Well... I was very shocked to be seeing all this, of course...

Japanese: ちょっと、ハッキリしないけど‥‥
English: So I'm not entirely sure...

Japanese: 血ィは、見なかったと思うわ。 ‥‥そのときは。
English: But I don't think I saw any blood. Not then.

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、 そうだったでしょうね。
English: ...I'm sure that you didn't.

Japanese: 凶器は、出血をふせぐ“フタ”の 役割をしていたのだから。
English: The weapon was acting as a plug in the wound.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥、最初に ハッキリさせておこう。
English: In any case... Let's be clear on one very important point.

Japanese: 証人は、目撃したのだろうか？ 被害者がカタナで刺される瞬間を！
English: Did you actually see the instant in which the victim was stabbed!?

Japanese: ‥‥よく思い出していただきたい。 重要なコト、だ。
English: ...Think carefully. This is very important.

Japanese: あやめのコトですからねぇ。 一生懸命、考えてますよ。
English: It's Iris we are talking about here! I'm thinking for all I'm worth!

Japanese: やっぱり、オバサンが見たときは もう、刺さってたねぇ。カタナ。
English: No, when I looked over, the sword was already in Mystic Elise's body.

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥
English: Hmmmmmm...

Japanese: （決定的とは言えないが‥‥）
English: (It might not be conclusive, but...)

Japanese: （彼女の主張を ウラづける証言だな‥‥）
English: (...this testimony supports her theory.)

Japanese: 被害者は、自室で刺されてから、 境内に転落した‥‥
English: The victim was stabbed in her room and then dropped into the courtyard...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、 立証されたようね、御剣　怜侍。
English: ...I think this proves it rather well, Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥被害者の血を 目撃した、と‥‥？
English: ...So you're saying that you saw the victim's blood?

Japanese: そ、そうなの！　あの。 あ、あやめにも少し、返り血が‥‥
English: T-That's right! Some of it had splattered onto Iris, too...

Japanese: 被告人・あやめは、逮捕されたとき 自室でメイソウをしていた。
English: When the defendant was arrested, she was meditating in her room.

Japanese: 返り血で汚れた装束は、キチンと たたんで、わきに置いてあったわ。
English: And her blood-flecked clothing was neatly folded in the corner.

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！ （返り血をあびた、装束が‥‥）
English: Wh-What!? (Her clothes were blood- flecked as well...?)

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥やはり、 決定的なようですなあ‥‥。
English: Hmmm... That seems quite conclusive to me...

Japanese: （どうする‥‥？ さらに追求するべきか‥‥）
English: (What should I do...? Press this point further?)

Japanese: （‥‥これ以上、被告人に不利な 状況になっては、勝ち目なし）
English: (I can't afford to make things look any worse for the defendant.)

Japanese: （ここは、ようすを見ておく べきだろう‥‥）
English: (I'll bide my time for now.)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。 証言をつづけていただきましょう。
English: ...Witness. Please continue your testimony.

Japanese: 被害者の出血を目撃した、 そのあとのことを！
English: From after you witnessed the victim bleeding!

Japanese: ‥‥先ほどの話にもあったが‥‥
English: Going back to your previous statement...

Japanese: 刺された瞬間の出血量は、それほど たいしたものではないという。
English: ...you said that you saw little bleeding when the victim was stabbed.

Japanese: しかし‥‥あなたは、被害者の “出血”を目撃したそうだが‥‥？
English: But now... you say you saw the victim bleeding...?

Japanese: あらあら。オバサンだってねェ。 見るモンは見てるんだから。
English: Well, well. I say that what I saw is what I saw.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですかな？
English: W-What did you see?

Japanese: たしかにオバサン、カタナを刺す ところは見なかったけど‥‥
English: Maybe I didn't see the poor woman get stabbed...

Japanese: あの子が、カタナを抜くところは ハッキリ見ましたからねえ。
English: But I saw the girl pull the sword out of her, plain as day.

Japanese: カタナを‥‥“抜いた”‥‥？
English: Pulling the sword out...?

Japanese: まあ‥‥“抜いた”というより “抜けた”という感じだったけど。
English: Well, it wasn't exactly pulling... It was more like it "came out".

Japanese: 証人！　今の発言、 証言に加えていただきましょう！
English: Witness! You will add this statement to your testimony!

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。 重要なコトだったのかしら‥‥？
English: Oh... Was that important...!?

Japanese: （‥‥あなたが 想像もつかないほどに‥‥）
English: (More important than you can imagine...)

Japanese: 出血は、《ハンニン》が カタナを抜いたため、だろうか‥‥
English: Was the bleeding caused by the killer removing the sword...?

Japanese: ちがいないね！　あのときのコト、 オバサン、バッチリ思い出したよ！
English: No mistaking it! I remember it all, clear as day!

Japanese: ‥‥妙だ、とは思いませんか？
English: ...Don't you think that's a bit odd?

Japanese: どういうことかしら？ 御剣　怜侍。
English: What do you mean, Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: 《ハンニン》は‥‥なぜ、 カタナを抜いたのだろうか？
English: Why would the killer pull out the sword?
What...?

Japanese: 被害者をつらぬいた凶器を、 黄金像に持たせるだけならば‥‥
English: If all she wanted to do was place the weapon in the hand of the gold statue...

Japanese: なにも‥‥ 死体からカタナを抜く必要はない。
English: ...then there was no reason to remove it from the body.

Japanese: そんなことをすれば、 いたずらに出血するだけだ。
English: All that would've accomplished is causing the victim to bleed unnecessarily.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがかな？　狩魔検事。
English: ...Your thoughts, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: T-That's...

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ 妙に、しめやかですね‥‥。
English: Indeed... It is strange, now that you mention it.

Japanese: （いずれ、そのナゾにも 答えを出さねばなるまい‥‥）
English: (I'm going to need to find an answer to this mystery, too...)

Japanese: と、とにかく！　証人は、 ひどい光景を目撃した。
English: A-Anyway! The witness saw something terrible!

Japanese: ‥‥それから、 どうなったのかしら？
English: ...So what did you do after that, witness?

Japanese: それでは‥‥そのあと 《ハンニン》が何をしたか‥‥
English: Now, on to what the killer did next...

Japanese: あなたは見ていない、 ということだろうか？
English: You saw none of it? Correct?

Japanese: まあ‥‥キゼツしてたワケ ですからねぇ。
English: Well... I was unconscious...

Japanese: 気を失っていたのは、 どれぐらいのあいだかしら？
English: How long were you out for?

Japanese: さあ‥‥１０分か２０分 ぐらいだと思うケド。
English: I don't know... 10, maybe 20 minutes...?

Japanese: 若いギザギザ頭の男のコに 起こされたんですよ。
English: A young man with a very prickly looking head woke me up.

Japanese: ‥‥ドテっぱらをギュウって 踏みにじられて。
English: ...By stepping on me, actually.

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥その 起こし方は、どうかと思いますね。
English: Hmm... I'm not sure I like that method of resuscitation!

Japanese: ま。そりゃ、オバサンも、 “せっぷん”までは言わないケド。
English: Well, I wasn't asking for mouth-to-mouth, or anything of the sort...

Japanese: ビジョを起こすなら、もうチョイ、 やさしくするべきよねぇ‥‥
English: ...but I would've welcomed a more gentle awakening right about then, let me tell you.

Japanese: ‥‥本人に、キツく言っておこう。
English: ...I shall have words with the offender personally.

Japanese: それで、目がさめたとき、 あなたが見たものは‥‥？
English: What did you see upon awakening...?

Japanese: “供子さま”というのは モチロン、黄金像ですね‥‥？
English: By "Mystic Ami", you are referring to the golden statue, correct?

Japanese: ヒトさまを宝刀・七支刀で 刺すだけでオソロシイのに‥‥
English: Just stabbing someone with the Shichishito, a sacred treasure, is terrible enough.

Japanese: そのカタナを、供子さまに お持たせするなんて‥‥！
English: But to then make Mystic Ami hold the blade...!

Japanese: まさに‥‥ニクむべき犯行ね。
English: Truly, a heinous, despicable crime.

Japanese: ‥‥ニクむのは、カンタンだ。 だれにでもできる。
English: It is easy to despise something. Anyone can do it.

Japanese: しかし。カンタンにできないコトが ひとつ、ある‥‥
English: However, there is something that cannot be done so easily...

Japanese: あなたにはムズカシイのでしょ。 ‥‥何かしら？　御剣　怜侍。
English: Whatever it is, I'm sure it's doubly hard for you. Anyway, what is the problem?

Japanese: ハンニンが“なぜ”‥‥ こんな細工をしたか‥‥？
English: Exactly why would the killer set up this gruesome scene?

Japanese: それを、合理的に 説明できるだろうか。
English: Can anyone explain the reasoning behind that?

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥ たしかに、思いつきませんな。
English: Hmmmmm... No, I don't think I can.

Japanese: ヒトはだれでも、その行動に 合理的な理由があるワケではない！
English: There isn't always a logical reason behind why someone acts!

Japanese: たしかに、そおよねぇ。 オバサンも、特に春先は‥‥
English: That's true, so true. In early spring, for example, I often find myself...

Japanese: （《ヒトはだれでも》‥‥ 便利なフレーズだな）
English: ("There isn't always"... That phrase might come in handy someday.)

Japanese: “スムーズに”‥‥ カタナが抜けるのを見た、と？
English: "Smoothly", you say... You're saying you saw the sword smoothly slide out?

Japanese: そおなのよねぇ‥‥。
English: That's right...

Japanese: あれは‥‥供子さまの黄金像の すぐそばだったわ。
English: The whole thing happened right next to the gold statue of Mystic Ami.

Japanese: エリスさまは倒れていて、 あやめが、かがみこんでいて。
English: Mystic Elise was on the ground, and Iris was stooped over her.

Japanese: カタナは、すっかり根本まで 刺さっていましたねぇ。
English: The sword was buried up to the hilt.

Japanese: あやめが立ち上がると、持って いたカタナも、すーって抜けて。
English: When Iris stood up, the sword in her hand just slid out of Mystic Elise's body.

Japanese: そ、その傷口から‥‥！ ううううう‥‥
English: It slid out from that gaping wound...! Aaaah...

Japanese: たしかに‥‥カタナが抜けたら、 出血したでしょうなあ‥‥。
English: It goes without saying... that if the sword was removed there would be bleeding.

Japanese: ‥‥なんのフシギもないわね。
English: ...Nothing out of place here.

Japanese: （そうだろうか‥‥）
English: (Is that really the case...?)

Japanese: （この証言‥‥何か、とても “不自然”な気がする‥‥）
English: (I can't help but feel that something about this testimony is very out of place...)

Japanese: （起こり得ないことが 起こっているような‥‥）
English: (That something which couldn't possibly have happened... appears to have happened.)

Japanese: 毘忌尼さん。 あなたは、信頼できる証人だ‥‥
English: Sister Bikini. You are a reliable witness...

Japanese: そう思いたいのだが‥‥ その証言に、ムジュンが多すぎる。
English: At least, I'd like to think so. But there are too many contradictions here.

Japanese: な、なんなのよ！ ヒトをウソつきみたいにィィ！
English: W-What do you mean? You make it sound as though I'm a liar!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ‥‥。ちょっと イイ男だから、ユルすけど。
English: But... You're a handsome young man, so I'll forgive you.

Japanese: “ムジュン”‥‥ どういうことかしら？
English: What contradictions are you talking about?

Japanese: 証人が見たと主張する 光景では‥‥
English: In the scene that the witness claims to have seen...

Japanese: 凶器は、その根本まで 被害者をつらぬいていたという。
English: ...the weapon was thrust up to its hilt into the victim.

Japanese: さらに‥‥ハンニンは、その凶器を スムーズに死体から抜いている。
English: Furthermore... the killer withdrew the weapon smoothly from the body.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！ そのいずれも、あり得ないのだ。
English: ...However! Both of these are complete impossibilities!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコトかな‥‥？ 説明をおねがいしま
English: What do you mean...? Please, explain you...

Japanese: うははははははははははッ！
English: Uwaaaaah!

Japanese: どういうコトなの！ 説明しなさいッ！
English: Explain yourself!

Japanese: そもそも‥‥こんな凶器を、根本 まで刺すことができるだろうか？
English: To start with... Do you think it would be possible to stab someone to the hilt with this?

Japanese: 被告人は、それほどチカラのある 女性には見えないが‥‥？
English: No matter how I look at the defendant, she doesn't appear strong enough for that.

Japanese: “そうは見えない”‥‥ そんなコトバは無力よ。
English: "Doesn't appear"...? What meaningless dribble!

Japanese: 私だって、見た目は このとおり、か弱いけれど‥‥
English: I, too, may appear to be weak and frail...

Japanese: その気になれば、オトコの ひとりやふたり、泣かせてみせる！
English: But I can crush men under my heel and make them weep, should I so choose!

Japanese: 異議を認めます！
English: The objection stands!

Japanese: さっき、この私ですら、ちょっと 泣いてしまいましたからな。
English: I wept a little back there, I must admit.

Japanese: ‥‥モンダイは、 それだけではない。
English: ...That isn't the only issue here.

Japanese: もし、このカタナが根本まで 刺さってしまえば‥‥
English: If this sword was truly stabbed into the body up to the hilt...

Japanese: これだけ枝分かれしているのだ。 “スムーズ”に抜けるハズがない！
English: Well, just look at all the branches on it. It certainly wouldn't come out smoothly.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥‥
English: T-That's...

Japanese: それに、出血量の問題がある。
English: We also have the problem of the amount of bleeding.

Japanese: カタナで刺された場合、その凶器が “フタ”になることは考えられる。
English: It's true that when a blade is left in a body, it acts as a "plug" of sorts.

Japanese: しかし！　このようなフクザツな カタチとなれば、話はベツだ。
English: However! When the weapon is shaped like this, it's an entirely different story.

Japanese: 傷口は広がり、出血は おさえられなかっただろう！
English: The wound would be too large for the blade to completely stop from bleeding!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そんなもの‥‥ スイソクにすぎないわ！
English: T-That's nothing more than conjecture!

Japanese: 現に、被害者は七支刀で 刺されているのよ。
English: In reality, the victim was stabbed with the Shichishito.

Japanese: こんなカタチの凶器でも‥‥
English: Even a weapon of this nature...

Japanese: スムーズに抜けることもあるし、 出血を止めることもあるッ！
English: ...may still sometimes slide out smoothly, and may still sometimes stop the blood loss!

Japanese: それだけではないのだよ。
English: I'm not finished.

Japanese: さらにもう１つ‥‥ 決定的なムジュンがある。
English: There is still one more... conclusive contradiction.

Japanese: ま‥‥まだ、あるのですか！
English: Y-You've still got more!?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなコトだ。
English: ...This one is simple.

Japanese: もし、このカタナが 根本まで刺さったのであれば‥‥
English: If this sword really was thrust in all the way to the hilt...

Japanese: なぜ、その血痕が、カタナの 切っ先にしか残っていないのか？
English: ...why is there only blood on the tip of it?

Japanese: ‥‥もし、この証人の 証言が正しいのならば！
English: If this witness is telling the truth...

Japanese: 刀身の全体に、痕跡が 残っているはずではないかッ！
English: Then there should be blood along the entire length of the sword!

Japanese: ぐうううううううッ！
English: Noooooo!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛
English: Order! Order! Ord--

Japanese: うはははははははははははッ！
English: Whaaaaaaaa!

Japanese: さすがは‥‥御剣　怜侍ね。
English: Bravo... Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: たった１本の凶器から、ここまでの ムジュン点をすくい上げるなんて。
English: Raising this many contradictions from a single piece of evidence!

Japanese: 私の知っている弁護士なら‥‥ おそらく、１コがせいぜい。
English: All the other attorneys I know could maybe manage one, if that!

Japanese: しかし！　これはいったい、 どういうことなのですか！
English: But what does this all mean!?

Japanese: 凶器に、多くのムジュンが あることはわかりましたが‥‥
English: You have proven contradictions regarding the murder weapon, but...

Japanese: ‥‥そこまでわかれば、 コタエは１つしかなかろう。
English: ...Having come this far, there can only be one answer.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥‥？
English: And that is...?

Japanese: 被害者のイノチをうばった 《凶器》は‥‥
English: The weapon used to kill the victim...

Japanese: このカタナ、 《七支刀》ではなかったのだッ！
English: ...was not the Shichishito!

Japanese: な‥‥なんとォォォッ！
English: W-Whaaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: バカの、バカによる、 バカ中心的でバカげた考えね‥‥
English: A foolishly foolish idea born from the foolish mind of a foolhardy foolish fool.

Japanese: そもそも‥‥これが凶器だと 考えられたのは、なぜだろうか？
English: Let's examine this again. What was it that made us think this sword was the murder weapon?

Japanese: そ、それは、モチロン‥‥
English: W-Well...

Japanese: 供子さまが‥‥その手に、 お持ちになっていたから‥‥
English: It's because Mystic Ami was holding it...

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。 しかし！
English: ...Exactly. However!

Japanese: 逆に考えれば、コンキョは それだけなのだ。
English: If you reflect on this, that is the only basis we have to assume such a thing.

Japanese: ‥‥あの光景の印象が強すぎた。 それゆえ、我々は思いこんだ。
English: The impression left by the scene was just too strong. That is what influenced us.

Japanese: この七支刀こそが “凶器である”‥‥と！
English: It influenced us to believe that the Shichishito was the murder weapon!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ うはははははははははははッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! Waaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: “凶器が《七支刀》では なかった”‥‥
English: So maybe the Shichishito was not the murder weapon.

Japanese: もし、そうだったとしても‥‥ 何も変わらないわ、御剣　怜侍！
English: Even if that is the case... it changes nothing, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: ジューショクさまは、 目撃しているのだから！
English: The Sister here saw everything!

Japanese: 被告人が《カタナのようなもの》で 被害者を刺しつらぬいた光景を！
English: She saw the defendant stab the victim with a sword-like object.

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥ それは、そうですな！
English: Hmmmmmm... That's true!

Japanese: いかがですかな、弁護人！
English: Your response, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: それならば‥‥検察側に、当然の ギモンの答えをいただきたい。
English: If that is so... I would like the prosecution to answer the obvious question it raises.

Japanese: 当然の‥‥ギモン？
English: The obvious... question?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。すなわち‥‥
English: ...Yes. Namely...

Japanese: ホンモノの凶器は いったい、どこへ消えたのか？
English: Where did the real murder weapon disappear to?

Japanese: 当然、警察は葉桜院内、および その周辺を捜査したはずだ。
English: It goes without saying that the police searched the Main Hall and the surrounding area.

Japanese: ‥‥《カタナのようなもの》は 発見されたのかな？
English: ...Perhaps the prosecution can enlighten us as to if a "sword-like object" was found.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥‥
English: T-That's...

Japanese: どうなのですか！　狩魔検事！
English: Answer the question, Ms. von Karma!

Japanese: それらしき証拠品は 発見されていないわ‥‥
English: No evidence of that kind was found.

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥ また１つ、ナゾが増えたようです。
English: Hmmmmm... Another mystery to throw onto the pile!

Japanese: 凶器が発見されないとなると、 審理はヤッカイなことに‥‥
English: A trial without a murder weapon is a tricky beast!

Japanese: あの‥‥ ちょっといいかしらねェ。
English: Excuse me... Could I say something...?

Japanese: オバサン、思い出した コトがあるんだけど‥‥
English: I just remembered something, actually...

Japanese: ‥‥何かしら？ ジューショクさま‥‥
English: ...What is it, Sister?

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥
English: I was just thinking... It's possible...

Japanese: アレは、ああいうコト だったのかもしれないねぇ‥‥
English: ...that just maybe... what actually happened was... it was just over there...

Japanese: ゼンゼン、わかりません。
English: What exactly are you going on aboot here?

Japanese: キョーキよ、キョーキ！ ‥‥もしかしたら‥‥
English: The murder weapon, I mean! Maybe...

Japanese: オバサン、知ってるかも！ カタナを捨てた場所をッ！
English: I think I might know where the sword was disposed of!

Japanese: なんだと‥‥ッ！
English: You what!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: Well then...

Japanese: ジューショクさまの証言‥‥ もう一度、聞く必要があるようね。
English: I think we need to hear testimony from you one more time, Sister.
(Impossible...!)

Japanese: （この上‥‥いったい、何を見たと 言うのだ、このオバサンは！）
English: (What else... What else could this old woman have seen!?)

Japanese: ～“凶器”のゆくえ～
English: -- Location of the Weapon --

Japanese: オバサンが、事件を目撃したのは １１時ごろだったワケだけど‥‥ 11 PM...
English: I saw the murder at around

Japanese: 事件の通報をたのんだあと、 山門のトコロに出てみたの。
English: And after asking that it be reported, I went out to the Main Gate.

Japanese: そこに‥‥スノーモービルが 走ったアトが残っていたの！
English: And there... I saw tracks! Tracks that indicated the snowmobile had been used!

Japanese: おぼろ橋まで歩くと１５分だけど、 あれを使えば、５分もかからない！
English: It takes 15 minutes to walk to Dusky Bridge, but less than 5 using one of those!

Japanese: オバサンがキゼツしたあと、凶器を 吾童川に捨ててきたんじゃない？
English: Maybe they threw the weapon into Eagle River and came back while I was knocked out?

Japanese: ‥‥あやめなら、それができた。 スノーモービル、運転できるし‥‥
English: ...Iris could have done that. She can drive a snowmobile after all...

Japanese: ぬうううううううむ‥‥
English: Hmmmmmm...

Japanese: 証人。証言を、小出しに しないようにおねがいします。
English: Witness. Please, tell us everything you know right away next time.

Japanese: ま。オバサンもアレよね。 コシがアレだし、トシもアレだし。
English: Well. I'm not in the best of shape. What with my back and my age, you know.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥
English: Quite...

Japanese: 山門の前には、スノーモービルの シュプールが残っていたわ。
English: There were indeed snowmobile tracks in front of the Main Gate.

Japanese: これが、その写真よ。
English: Here is a photograph.

Japanese: スノーモービル‥‥ですか。 ‥‥あり得ますねえ‥‥。
English: A snowmobile, eh... I see. Well, it certainly is an interesting theory...

Japanese: シュプールは、葉桜院の前から 始まって‥‥
English: The tracks begin in front of Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: おぼろ橋の前まで つながっていたわ！
English: ...and run all the way to Dusky Bridge!

Japanese: 証拠品《シュプールの写真》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Tracks Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥これで、問題ないようね。
English: ...That solves your pesky little problem, yes?

Japanese: 吾童川は、流れが早いため、 冬でも凍結することはない。
English: The Eagle River's current is quite swift, meaning that it doesn't freeze over in winter.

Japanese: 凶器を捨てるには、まさに 絶好のポイントよ！
English: Making it the perfect place to dispose of the murder weapon!

Japanese: （凶器を‥‥川へ捨てに行った？）
English: (Did she really go to the river to dispose of the murder weapon...?)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人！ ‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Edgeworth! ...Your cross-examination, please.

Japanese: ～“凶器”のゆくえ～
English: -- Location of the Weapon --

Japanese: オバサンが、事件を目撃したのは １１時ごろだったワケだけど‥‥ 11 PM...
English: I saw the murder at around

Japanese: 事件の通報をたのんだあと、 山門のトコロに出てみたの。
English: And after asking that it be reported, I went out to the Main Gate.

Japanese: そこに‥‥スノーモービルが 走ったアトが残っていたの！
English: And there... I saw tracks! Tracks that indicated the snowmobile had been used!

Japanese: おぼろ橋まで歩くと１５分だけど、 あれを使えば、５分もかからない！
English: It takes 15 minutes to walk to Dusky Bridge, but less than 5 using one of those!

Japanese: オバサンがキゼツしたあと、凶器を 吾童川に捨ててきたんじゃない？
English: Maybe they threw the weapon into Eagle River and came back while I was knocked out?

Japanese: ‥‥あやめなら、それができた。 スノーモービル、運転できるし‥‥
English: ...Iris could have done that. She can drive a snowmobile after all...

Japanese: （また１つ、不可解なナゾが 増えてしまったようだ‥‥）
English: (Another mystery to feed the fire...)

Japanese: （“凶器など、捨てに行く 必要があったのか”‥‥？）
English: ("Was there any reason to go and throw away the murder weapon"...?)

Japanese: （‥‥さいわい、この証言には あからさまな食いちがいがある）
English: (Luckily, there is surely a problem with this testimony.)

Japanese: （まずは、そこを 打ちくずすとするか‥‥）
English: (Now all I have to do is start poking holes in this flawed account...)

Japanese: ‥‥その時間に まちがいはないだろうか？
English: ...You are sure about the time?

Japanese: そおねぇ‥‥オバサン、 時間は気にしていたから。
English: Yes... I was worried about it, after all.

Japanese: それはまた、なぜですかな？
English: Why was that?

Japanese: だってオバサン、セキニン感が 強いから。特に、今の季節は。
English: Because I have a strong sense of responsibility. Especially at this time of year.

Japanese: 修験者さまが冷たい宝水を ひっかぶってるのに‥‥
English: The acolyte was being doused in freezing water at the time...

Japanese: いつまでも、おフロに入ってる ワケにもいかないでしょお？
English: I couldn't very well take it easy in the bath all night now, could I?

Japanese: だから、１１時には葉桜院を 出よう、って思ってたのよねぇ。
English: So at 11, I decided to leave Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥時間に関しては 信頼してよさそうだな）
English: (Her estimation of the time seems reliable, at least.)

Japanese: じゃあ、ジューショクさま。 つづきをおねがいするわ。
English: Please continue, Sister.

Japanese: “通報をたのんだ” ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ですね？
English: You asked Phoenix Wright to report the crime, correct?

Japanese: そうそうそう。オバサンの ドテっぱら踏んづけた、あの子。
English: Right, right, the one who trampled me.

Japanese: （根に持つタイプの オバサンだな‥‥）
English: (It seems she is the type to hold a grudge...)

Japanese: 葉桜院に電話がないから、橋の たもとまで行ってもらったの。
English: There isn't a phone in the Main Hall, so I sent him to the bridge.

Japanese: ‥‥あの弁護士‥‥ 携帯電話も持っていないのかしら。
English: Phoenix Wright... He didn't even have his cell phone on him?

Japanese: たまたま忘れたそうだ。
English: He had forgotten it at home, apparently.

Japanese: あいかわらず、アマいオトコね。
English: What a naïve boy, as always!

Japanese: 私など、電話の他に、ムチまで キチンと携帯してるというのに。
English: Not only do I always carry my phone, but I always have my whip in hand, too!

Japanese: とにかく、オバサン‥‥ なんか、コワくなって。
English: Anyway, I was really scared... And it was taking him a while to get back...

Japanese: 早く帰ってこないかな、って 山門のところに出たワケね。
English: So I thought I'd go out by the Main Gate for a spell.

Japanese: たしか‥‥山門の前に一台、 スノーモービルがあったが‥‥
English: As I recall, there was a snowmobile outside the Main Gate when I visited.

Japanese: 葉桜院にあるのは、 あれ一台きりよぉ。
English: That's it. That's the only one we have.

Japanese: どんなに雪が降っても、 ガリガリ走るんだから！
English: It'll run no matter how much snow falls!

Japanese: そのスノーモービルは、 ちゃんと、門の前に‥‥？
English: Now, you're certain the snowmobile was there at the Main Gate when you arrived?

Japanese: あ。モチロン、あのときは 門の前に、とめてあったわよぉ。
English: Yes, of course. It was parked in front of the gate.

Japanese: つまり‥‥そのときはすでに、 凶器を捨ててきた後、ということ。
English: So... she had already gone, discarded the murder weapon, and returned by that time.

Japanese: （‥‥微妙な問題だが‥‥ どうする‥‥？）
English: (...I'm not sure if this is really relevant... What should I do...?)

Japanese: （‥‥どんなギモンにも、 答えは出しておくべきだろう）
English: (...I need answers to every possible doubt.)

Japanese: そのスノーモービルだが‥‥ まだ、あたたかかっただろうか？
English: The snowmobile in question... Was it still warm at that time?

Japanese: え？　え？　え？　え？ どういうこと？　どういうこと？
English: Huh? Huh? Huh? Huh? What do you mean? What do you mean?

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean, eh?

Japanese: どういうこと？　御剣　怜侍！
English: What do you mean, Miles Edgeworth!?

Japanese: （‥‥カンの悪い連中だな‥‥）
English: (...I'm playing to a slow crowd here.)

Japanese: スノーモービルが使用されれば、 エンジンが熱を持つはずだ。
English: It goes without saying that using a snowmobile will heat its engine.

Japanese: まだあたたかければ、使用されて “間”がなかったことになる。
English: If it was still warm, then it means it was recently used.

Japanese: あ！　なるほどねー！ そりゃ気がつかなかったワ！
English: Ah! I see! I never thought of that!

Japanese: ぬううううううううむ、たしかに！ それは見落としていました！
English: Hmmm, that's right! I overlooked that, too!

Japanese: ‥‥トーゼンね。
English: ...Of course you did.

Japanese: それで‥‥質問の答えは、 いかがなものか？
English: Then answer the question please, witness.

Japanese: オバサン、スノーモービルを さわりに行ったワケじゃないから。
English: I don't often go around touching hot engines...

Japanese: ‥‥むむぅ。
English: ...Hmm.

Japanese: でもね。そういえば‥‥
English: However... Now that you mention it...

Japanese: 雪が、つもってなかったわ。
English: There wasn't any snow on it.

Japanese: 雪が‥‥？
English: Snow...?

Japanese: ‥‥なぜか、 スノーモービルの上だけ‥‥
English: Yes. For some reason, only the snowmobile wasn't covered in snow...

Japanese: （‥‥雪が‥‥ つもっていなかった！）
English: (There wasn't any snow on it!? Curses!)

Japanese: やはり‥‥犯人はスノーモービルを 使用したようです‥‥
English: It seems highly likely that the killer did use the snowmobile then, eh!

Japanese: それを使うと‥‥おぼろ橋まで、 どれぐらいかかるのかしら？
English: How long does it take to get to Dusky Bridge by snowmobile?

Japanese: （おそらく‥‥スノーモービルは 事件に関係があるのだろう）
English: (It looks very likely that the snowmobile is related to this case.)

Japanese: （あやめさんが、それを使った 可能性は‥‥高い気がする）
English: (I can't help but think that Iris used it... But for what...?)

Japanese: （ここは‥‥ 深入りしないほうがいい）
English: (I'd better hold off on this... for now.)

Japanese: ‥‥それで、証人。
English: ...Well then, witness.

Japanese: スノーモービルを使うと、橋まで どれぐらいかかるのですかな？
English: How long does it take to reach the bridge by snowmobile?

Japanese: それならば‥‥なぜ、あなたは 使用しなかったのだろうか？
English: In that case... why didn't you use it yourself? You could've spared yourself some walking.

Japanese: それ！　それなのよねェ。 ちょっと、聞いてくれます？
English: Ah! There's a reason for that! Have we got a moment for me to explain?

Japanese: まあ‥‥そのために、あなたを かこんでいるワケですからね。
English: I think that's why the question was asked in the first place...

Japanese: ‥‥あれは、１ヶ月ぐらい 前のコトでしたか。
English: ...It was about a month ago.

Japanese: オバサン、ゴキゲンで 愛車を乗り回してたんですけど‥‥
English: I was driving my beloved little snowmobile, happy as can be.

Japanese: 水くみの帰りに、思いっきり 木にゲキトツしちゃって！
English: I'd fetched some water and was heading back when I went and crashed into a tree!

Japanese: 木とオバサンのコシが、ミシッ、 といったワケ。ミシッ、て！
English: The tree and my back both went crunch. Just like that. Crunch.

Japanese: ぬううううううう‥‥ミシッ、と。
English: Hmm... Crunch.

Japanese: ‥‥それ以来、乗るのが コワくなっちゃってねェ。
English: ...I haven't been able to find the courage to ride anything since then...

Japanese: とにかく、ハンニンは アレを使って‥‥
English: Anyway, the killer must have used it!

Japanese: たしか、あなたが キゼツしていた時間は‥‥
English: Refresh our memory. How long were you knocked out for...?

Japanese: さっきも言ったけど、 １０分か、２０分ぐらいねぇ。
English: Like I said, somewhere between 10 to 20 minutes.

Japanese: スノーモービルを使えば、橋まで 往復で１０分ていど‥‥
English: It's possible to get to the bridge and back in 10 minutes using the snowmobile...

Japanese: ‥‥時間的には、じゅうぶん 可能、と言わざるを得ない。
English: ...I have to concede that is more than enough time.

Japanese: いかがかしら？　御剣　怜侍。
English: Is that all you wish to concede, Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: たしかに、“時間的には” 可能だっただろう‥‥。
English: While it would have been possible, time-wise...

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。やはりまた、 ひとつ“不自然”な点がある。
English: ...one element remains out of place here.

Japanese: どのあたりですかな？ ‥‥“不自然”というのは。
English: Oh? And what would this mystery element be?

Japanese: 《なぜ、ハンニンがそんなことを したか？》‥‥その理由だ。
English: The killer's reasoning, Your Honor. "Why did the killer do all of this?"

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、凶器を 吾童川まで捨てに行ったのか‥‥？
English: Why go to the Eagle River to dispose of the murder weapon when there are other methods?

Japanese: ぬううううううう‥‥ 不可解なコトが多いですなあ。
English: Hmm... Too many unanswered questions!

Japanese: いかがですか、狩魔検事！
English: Your response, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: うははははははははははははッ！
English: Waaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ちょっとわからないと、すぐ この私をたよりにして‥‥
English: Turning to me for help over the slightest thing!

Japanese: たまには自分で考えたら どうなの、裁判長！
English: Why don't you think for yourself once in a while? ...Your Honor.

Japanese: えええええッ！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: （‥‥あいかわらず ムチャなオンナだ‥‥）
English: (She's as over the top as always...)

Japanese: と、とにかく‥‥！
English: A-Anyway...

Japanese: 理由はどうあれ、被告人には それが可能だったのよ！
English: Whatever the reason, the fact remains that the defendant could have done this!

Japanese: 凶器は、川に捨てた！ ‥‥これでキマリね、御剣　怜侍。
English: The murder weapon was disposed of in the river! Another point to me, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥この写真は、 証明している。
English: I admit this photograph proves something.

Japanese: 事件当夜、スノーモービルが 使用されたことを。
English: It proves that the snowmobile was used on the night of the murder.

Japanese: 認めざるを得ないようね。 ‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: You've finally accepted the inevitable, it seems... Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: ただし‥‥。
English: However...

Japanese: それならば、なぜ。 シュプールは１本なのだろうか？
English: If what the witness says is true, why is there only one set of tracks?

Japanese: どういうこと‥‥かしら？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: 葉桜院から凶器を持ち出して、 川に捨てて戻ってきたら‥‥
English: Iris left Hazakura Temple, threw the weapon into the river, and then returned.

Japanese: 当然！　雪の上には ２本のシュプールがあるはず！
English: If this was the case, then naturally there should be two sets of tracks in the snow!

Japanese: 橋へ向かうときのものと、 橋から帰ってくるときのもの‥‥
English: Those from heading out to the bridge, and those from coming back.

Japanese: あ‥‥！ そりゃそうだわねぇ‥‥
English: Ah...! You're right!

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph...

Japanese: ひとつ、忘れていることが あるようね‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: You are forgetting one thing, Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 事件当夜は、雪が降っていたのよ。
English: On the night of the murder, it was snowing.

Japanese: 橋へ向かうシュプールは、 その雪が消してしまった‥‥
English: The tracks leading to the bridge were erased by the snowfall.

Japanese: そう考えれば、なんの ムジュンもないでしょ？
English: This removes your precious contradiction, now doesn't it!

Japanese: なるほど！　川に凶器を捨てている あいだに、雪がやんで‥‥
English: I see! While she was at the river, the snow stopped...

Japanese: 帰ってくるときのシュプールだけが 雪の上に残されたワケですか‥‥
English: ...leaving just the return tracks in the snow.

Japanese: さあ、いかがかしら！ ‥‥御剣　怜侍！
English: What do you have to say now, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: （‥‥狩魔検事の主張‥‥ オカシイところは、ないか？）
English: (Is there a flaw in her theory...?)

Japanese: （“雪がシュプールを消した” ‥‥という主張‥‥）
English: (This idea that the snowfall covered one set of tracks...?)

Japanese: ‥‥橋へ向かうシュプールが 雪に消された‥‥
English: ...The tracks leading to the bridge were covered by the snow.

Japanese: その可能性を否定する材料は、 弁護側にはないようだ。
English: I do not have any evidence that can dispute this claim.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば、あとは カンタンな話よ。
English: ...In which case, the rest is simple.

Japanese: ジューショクさま。
English: Sister.

Japanese: あの夜、スノーモービルを 運転できたのは、だれかしら？
English: Who was the only person who could have driven the snowmobile that night?

Japanese: それは‥‥その‥‥‥！
English: Well... that would be...!

Japanese: ‥‥かくしてもダメ。 調べはついているのよ。
English: ...You can't hide it. I already know the answer.

Japanese: あやめ‥‥しかおりません。
English: It could only be Iris.

Japanese: そ‥‥その理由はッ！
English: A-And the reason being...!?

Japanese: スノーモービルのキーは １本しか、ないんです。
English: There is only one key for the snowmobile.

Japanese: そのキーは‥‥被告人が管理して いたことがわかっているわ。
English: A key... that the defendant is responsible for!

Japanese: なにィィィッ！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 今！　私にはゼンブ、 わかってしまったようです！
English: Now I don't feel like a clueless Puck Bunny! I understand everything!

Japanese: スノーモービルを使ったのが 被告人である以上‥‥
English: If the defendant did indeed use the snowmobile...

Japanese: やっぱり、犯人は、あやめさんで
English: ...which means Iris is most definitely the murder--

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！ お待ちいただこう。
English: ...Your Honor! Please, wait a moment.

Japanese: なんですか、御剣くん！
English: What is it, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 狩魔検事の主張には‥‥ １つ、大きなアナがあるッ！
English: There is one gaping hole in Ms. von Karma's claim!

Japanese: 弁護人。そういうコトは、 もっと早く言ってもらわないと。
English: I'm not sure if I care for your response, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥私は、どうかと思いますね！
English: Next time, bring up whatever issues you have earlier. Do you understand!?

Japanese: うむむむむむ‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: （‥‥最初から、気づく べきだったのだ‥‥）
English: (I should have noticed it sooner...!)

Japanese: “川へ向かうシュプールは、 雪によって消された”‥‥
English: The tracks to the river were covered by snow... What a nice theory.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。 ‥‥それは、あり得ないのだよ。
English: However, Ms. von Karma... That is impossible.

Japanese: その無礼につきつけられた、 人差し指‥‥
English: Would you care to explain...

Japanese: 説明してもらえるかしら？
English: ...why there is a rude index finger currently pointed in my general direction?

Japanese: ‥‥その必要はない。 証拠品が、すべてを語るだろう。
English: ...No need. The evidence will do all of the talking for me.

Japanese: 事件当夜、被告人は凶器を捨てる ため、おぼろ橋まで往復した。
English: On the night of the murder the killer went to, and returned from, Dusky Bridge...

Japanese: その“行き”のシュプールは、 雪によって消されてしまった‥‥
English: ...in order to dispose of the murder weapon. The outgoing tracks were erased by snow.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、検察側の主張です。
English: ...Or so claims Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 証拠を提示していただきましょう！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Present your evidence to the contrary, eh!

Japanese: “行き”のシュプールが、雪で 消されたのではないという証拠を！
English: Evidence that the outgoing tracks were not covered by snow!

Japanese: これが、その証拠品だッ！ いかがだろうか‥‥裁判長。
English: Here is the evidence! What do you have to say, Your Honor...?

Japanese: ‥‥１つだけ。 気になったことがあります。
English: ...This reminds me of when I was a little hockey goon in training...

Japanese: “行き”のシュプールを“雪”が 消した‥‥
English: When I was a child, I hoped for school to be canceled due to heavy snowfall.

Japanese: “ゆき”と“ゆき”をかけた おもしろさを狙っている‥‥
English: Perhaps, Mr. Edgeworth, it would've been better for you had court been snowed in.

Japanese: ‥‥そう受け取るべきなのですか？
English: ...That's all this evidence says to me, in any case.

Japanese: 他に、気になる点は ないのだろうか‥‥？
English: Nothing else...?

Japanese: ‥‥ありません。
English: Nothing else.

Japanese: まあ‥‥《ハズレ》 というコトね、御剣　怜侍。
English: Oh dear... You seem to have missed, Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: むぐ‥‥ッ！
English: Nngh...!

Japanese: （証人の証言によれば、事件が 起こったのは、１１時すぎ‥‥）
English: (According to the witness's testimony, the incident occurred after 11 PM...)

Japanese: （それならば‥‥）
English: (In which case...)

Japanese: （決定的に食いちがう 証拠があるはずだ‥‥！）
English: (...there has to be evidence that can undeniably prove something doesn't match up!)

Japanese: 証人！　たしか、あなたが 事件を目撃した時刻は‥‥？
English: Witness! Please tell us again what time it was when you witnessed the crime.

Japanese: さっきも言ったでしょ！ １１時ごろ‥‥でしたけど。
English: Like I said! It was around 11.

Japanese: ‥‥当然、凶器を捨てたのは それより“後”ということになる。
English: ...Of course, this means that the weapon was thrown away after that time, correct?

Japanese: そこで‥‥このデータを 見ていただきたい。
English: On that note... Please take a look at this data.

Japanese: 事件当夜の、吾童山中の 気象報告書だ。
English: It is the weather report for Eagle Mountain on the night of the murder.

Japanese: 気象‥‥報告、ですか‥‥
English: The... weather report...?

Japanese: 午後７時より降り始めた雪は‥‥ １０時５０分ごろ、やんでいる。
English: Snow started to fall at 7 PM... But it stopped at around 10:50.

Japanese: つまり！　証人が目撃した １１時には‥‥ 11 PM...
English: Therefore! When the Sister witnessed the crime at

Japanese: すでに雪はやんでいたことになる！
English: ...the snow had already stopped falling!

Japanese: シュプールが消されることなど、 あり得ないのだッ！
English: It is impossible for any tracks made after that time to have been covered up!

Japanese: くくううぅッ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: Very well then...

Japanese: 検察側の主張は、“雪”によって 消されてしまったようですな‥‥
English: It looks like Ms. von Karma's claim has been... snowed in.

Japanese: うはははははははははははははッ！
English: Waaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥キレイにまとめるのは まだ早いわ、裁判長‥‥。
English: ...It's too soon to be closing this trial due to snow!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍！　お天気に たよるとは、情けない‥‥
English: Miles Edgeworth! How pathetic of you to rely on the weather of all things!

Japanese: ここイチバンというときに、 たよりにならない。
English: Answer me this, then!

Japanese: それが、天気予報よ。
English: When is a weather report ever correct!?

Japanese: い、いやいや。しかし‥‥
English: Ah, no, no no... You've got it all wrong.

Japanese: これは予報ではなくて、 ジッサイのデータですから‥‥
English: This isn't a forecast... This is... actual data...

Japanese: ぎゃッほお！
English: Gyaaaaah!

Japanese: お天気の記録には、どうしても 誤差が生まれてしまうもの‥‥
English: Forecast, data, all weather reports have some inaccuracies!

Japanese: 現場付近では、１１時をすぎても、 まだ雪が降っていたかもしれない！
English: It may have still been snowing in the vicinity well past 11 PM!

Japanese: ぬうううううううう‥‥ たしかに、断言はできませんな。
English: Hmmmm... It's true. We cannot be totally sure, eh!

Japanese: な、なんだと！ （そんな、テキトーな‥‥）
English: W-What!? (How did she pull that off!?)

Japanese: 『事件があったとき、葉桜院では すでに、雪はやんでいた』‥‥
English: "It had stopped snowing at Hazakura Temple when the murder took place."

Japanese: ‥‥それを、決定的に 立証する証拠が必要です。
English: ...You need to provide conclusive evidence of this.

Japanese: （ここまで来て‥‥あとに ひけるワケがない‥‥）
English: (I've come this far... There's no turning back now.)

Japanese: ‥‥よかろう。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: 弁護側としても、立証に ギモンの余地は残したくない。
English: I, too, cannot allow any doubt to remain concerning this testimony.

Japanese: フッ‥‥タチの悪い カンペキ主義ね‥‥御剣　怜侍！
English: Hah... You can't back down, can you? Such a perfectionist, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: それでは‥‥弁護人！　証拠品を 提示していただきましょう！
