English: Very well then... Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: 被害者が殺害されたとき‥‥ すでに雪がやんでいた証拠を！
English: Where is your evidence that it had already stopped snowing when the victim was killed!?

Japanese: いかがですか、狩魔検事。
English: Your response, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: It looks like...

Japanese: あなたのココロには、まだ 雪が降っているようね。
English: It is still snowing in your heart, too.

Japanese: その雪にふるえ、 目をふさがれている‥‥
English: Shivering in the cold, you are closing your eyes to the truth...

Japanese: ‥‥さすが、わざわざアメリカから 来ただけのことはある。
English: And to think you just arrived after a long, tiring flight from Germany.

Japanese: なかなか、ウマいことを 言いますね。
English: That was poetry. Pure poetry.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥この証拠は 冷え切っていたようだな‥‥）
English: (This evidence is only going to freeze my case solid.)

Japanese: いかがかしら？　御剣　怜侍。
English: Respond! Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥たとえ、私のココロに雪が ふりつもろうとも‥‥
English: Even if it is snowing in my heart...

Japanese: 事件当時！　‥‥葉桜院の雪は すでに、やんでいたはずなのだ！
English: At the time of the murder... it had already stopped snowing at Hazakura Temple!

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョクのところ、 ポイントは、たった１つ‥‥だ。
English: Ultimately, it all comes down to one point.

Japanese: すなわち‥‥
English: That being...

Japanese: 被害者が刺された瞬間、境内に 雪が降っていたかどうか‥‥？
English: Whether or not it was snowing in that courtyard when the victim was stabbed...

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことね。 しかし、それを立証するのは‥‥
English: That's right. But proving that is...

Japanese: きわめて、たやすい。
English: Incredibly easy.

Japanese: 境内の状況を知りたければ‥‥ 写真を見ればよい。
English: If we want to know whether it was snowing or not, this photo will tell us everything.

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。 《現場写真》だ、モチロン。
English: Of course, I am referring to the photo of the crime scene.

Japanese: ごらんのとおり‥‥すべてが 雪でおおわれている。
English: As you can see... everything is covered with snow.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ１つのものをのぞいて。
English: ...With just one exception.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: And that is...?

Japanese: 被害者・天流斎　エリスだ。
English: The victim herself, Ms. Elise Deauxnim!

Japanese: なぜ、彼女の上にだけ、 雪がつもっていないのか‥‥？
English: Why is there no snow on top of her...?

Japanese: 答えは、わかりきっている。
English: The answer is simple!

Japanese: 彼女が殺害されたとき、 雪はすでにやんでいたッ！
English: It had stopped snowing when she was killed, that's why!
Gnnngh!

Japanese: つまり！
English: In other words!

Japanese: 犯行のあと、犯人が吾童川に 凶器を捨てに行ったのであれば‥‥
English: If the killer really did go to the Eagle River to dispose of the murder weapon...

Japanese: この写真にはやはり！　シュプール は２本、残っているハズなのだッ！
English: ...then in this photograph, there should be two sets of tracks!

Japanese: きゃああああああっ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ こ、これはどういうことなの
English: Order! Order! Just what are you...

Japanese: ぎゃッほほお！
English: Gyahaah!

Japanese: い‥‥いったい‥‥ どういうことなの、御剣　怜侍！
English: J-Just what are you suggesting, Miles Edgeworth!?

Japanese: （正直なトコロ‥‥ 私にも、よくわからないが‥‥）
English: (To be honest... I am not entirely sure myself. But...)

Japanese: 事実を整理すると‥‥ こういうコトになる。
English: This is simply what all of the facts point to.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥スノーモービルを使い、 だれかが葉桜院をはなれた。
English: That night... someone used the snowmobile to leave Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: シュプールから見て、行き先は おぼろ橋と考えるべきだろう。
English: From the tracks left, it can be understood that they were heading for Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥ まだ、雪は降っていた。
English: At that time... it was still snowing.

Japanese: 当然ね。‥‥そのときの シュプールは消えているのだから。
English: Of course it was. Because those tracks were gone.

Japanese: そして‥‥その人物が 葉桜院に帰ってきたとき‥‥
English: Then when this person returned to Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: すでに雪はやんでいた。 だから、シュプールが残った。
English: ...the snow had stopped. Thus, the return tracks remained.

Japanese: ぬううううううううう‥‥む。
English: Hmmmmm...

Japanese: ちょっと、いいかしら？
English: Can I say something?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか、 オカシイ気がするのよねェ‥‥
English: This all sounds a bit fishy to me...

Japanese: ‥‥なにかしら？ ジューショクさま。
English: ...What does, Sister?

Japanese: スノーモービルのキーは‥‥ １本しか、ないんですよ。
English: There is only one key for the snowmobile.

Japanese: そして‥‥事件当夜、被告人が 持っていたことがわかっている。
English: Furthermore, on the night in question, we know that the defendant had it!

Japanese: 事件発生後、キーは 彼女の部屋から発見されたわ。
English: The key was found in her room after the murder!

Japanese: ‥‥だから、あの晩、 スノーモービルは‥‥
English: ...Which can only mean, that night...

Japanese: あやめが奥の院に行くときに 使ったことになりますよねえ‥‥
English: Iris used the snowmobile to go to the Inner Temple...

Japanese: （まあ‥‥彼女は奥の院には “行ってない”と言っているが）
English: (But... Iris said that she never went there...)

Japanese: （ここは、話を 聞いておくべき、だな‥‥）
English: (I should probably press on this point some more when I get the chance...)

Japanese: スノーモービルで吊り橋を わたることはできないから‥‥
English: The snowmobile can't cross the suspension bridge...

Japanese: あの子は橋のたもとに置いて、 奥の院に来たんでしょうねぇ。
English: So, she must have left it on the Hazakura side of the bridge and crossed on foot.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなりますね。
English: ...That sounds right.

Japanese: でも‥‥オバサンがあやめを残して 葉桜院に帰る途中‥‥
English: But... What's odd is, when I left Iris and returned to Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: おぼろ橋のたもとには‥‥ 何もなかったけどねぇ。
English: I didn't see anything near Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: そ‥‥それは、ジューショクさま。 きっと、見落としたのよ。
English: Y-You must have just failed to see it, Sister.

Japanese: それにねぇ。オバサンが 葉桜院に帰ってみると‥‥
English: Maybe... But when I made it back to Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 門のトコロに、ちゃんとあったの。 ‥‥あのスノーモービル。
English: ...it was there, by the Main Gate... The snowmobile, I mean.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいないわ。 すっぽり、雪がつもってたねぇ‥‥
English: I know what I saw. It was covered in snow, too...

Japanese: そ‥‥そのようなこと‥‥ あり得ないッ！
English: B-But that... isn't possible!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥これは、どういうことですか！
English: Order! Order! Order in the court! ...What does this all mean?

Japanese: ‥‥ううう‥‥
English: ...Nngh...

Japanese: いったい‥‥スノーモービルは、 なんのために使われたのか‥‥？
English: So then what was the snowmobile used for...?

Japanese: まず‥‥被告人が、奥の院に 行くときに使ったのではない。
English: It wasn't taken by the defendant when she went to the Inner Temple.

Japanese: それならば、証人が帰ってきた際、 山門の前にあるはずがないからだ。
English: If it had been, then the witness couldn't possibly have seen it by the gate.

Japanese: さらに‥‥犯行の後、凶器を 捨てに行ったわけでもない。
English: Furthermore... it wasn't used by the killer to dispose of the murder weapon.

Japanese: それならば‥‥この写真には、 シュプールが２本、あるはず‥‥
English: If that was the case, there should be two sets of tracks in this photo.

Japanese: しかし‥‥ これだけは、たしかだ。
English: All we know is this...

Japanese: 雪がやんだ後‥‥だれかが、 あれを使って葉桜院に戻った！
English: After it stopped snowing... someone used the snowmobile to return to Hazakura Temple!

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥
English: Hmmmmmm...

Japanese: （まさか‥‥スノーモービルが、 ここまで問題になるとは‥‥）
English: (I never thought a simple snowmobile could cause so much trouble...)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥この証人は ここまで、のようですね。
English: I think we've arrived at this point due to the witness...

Japanese: ええ、ええ。 そうみたいですねぇ‥‥
English: Yes, yes. I've nothing more to add.

Japanese: オバサンね。もお、ゼーンブ 話しちゃったから。知ってるコト。
English: I've told you everything, everything that I know.

Japanese: さて‥‥コマりましたな。 ‥‥どなたか‥‥
English: Well then... That still leaves us with the same problem.

Japanese: スノーモービルを目撃した 証人がいれば、いいのですが‥‥
English: If only there was someone... A witness who could testify to having seen the snowmobile!
(A witness, huh...)

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥おぼろ橋付近をサンポ していた関係者は、いませんかな？
English: Was there no one out, walking perhaps, near Dusky Bridge on that night?

Japanese: それはちょっと、ムリなハナシね。
English: I don't think that's likely.

Japanese: あの晩は、耳がモギ取れるほど 寒かったから‥‥
English: It was cold enough to freeze your ears off.

Japanese: オモテをほっつき歩くバカは いなかったと思いますよぉ。
English: Only an idiot would go out wandering in that.

Japanese: ‥‥よっぽどダイジな 用でもないかぎりは‥‥。
English: ...Unless they had something really important to do.

Japanese: ぬううううううううう‥‥ ザンネンですなあ。
English: Hmmmm... That's a shame.
(Hold on...)

Japanese: （どうも、ココロに引っかかる）
English: (Something is coming to me...)

Japanese: （あのサムい晩、オモテを ほっつき歩いていたバカ‥‥か）
English: (An idiot may well have gone wandering out on that sub-arctic night!)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: ...Your Honor!

Japanese: 私に‥‥ヒトリだけ、 思い当たる人物がある。
English: I actually have an idea... There may be one individual able to help us.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: R-Really!?

Japanese: ‥‥事件当夜、スノーモービルを 目撃した人物、かしら‥‥？
English: ...You know of someone who might have seen the snowmobile on the night of the murder...?

Japanese: それを見ているかどうかは わからない‥‥しかし。
English: I don't know for sure if he saw it or not...

Japanese: その人物について、２つ。 ‥‥思い当たることがある。
English: But there are two things about him that do come to mind.

Japanese: それは‥‥？
English: Which are...?

Japanese: １つ。‥‥その人物は事件当夜、 《トンでもないもの》を目撃した。
English: First... that he saw "something incredible" on the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥もう１つは？
English: ...And the second being?

Japanese: 私の知っている、その人物は‥‥
English: This individual that I am thinking of went wandering outside on that cold night...

Japanese: あのサムい晩、オモテをほっつき 歩くほどのバカだ、ということだ。
English: In other words, he is our kind of idiot.

Japanese: 弁護人！　その人物とは、 どなたですかッ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Who is this idiot you're talking aboot!?

Japanese: どういうことですかな、 ‥‥弁護人。
English: Just what is the meaning of this, Mr. Edgeworth...?

Japanese: その人物が‥‥あの晩、おぼろ橋 付近をほっつき歩いていた、と？
English: Are you saying that... this is the person who was near Dusky Bridge that night?

Japanese: そ、そんな気がしたのだが‥‥
English: I-I did think so, however...

Japanese: はうゥッ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: ほっつき歩くべきなのは あなたよ、御剣　怜侍‥‥
English: You're the wandering idiot, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: 少し、アタマを 冷やして来なさいな。
English: Cool off before you try to take me on again!

Japanese: うむむむむむ‥‥ （ナニをやっているのだ、私は！）
English: Ungh...! (What was I thinking!?)

Japanese: （アイツを証言台に引きずり出す、 絶好のチャンスではないか！）
English: (This is the perfect chance to drag him onto the stand!)

Japanese: ぬうううううううううう‥‥ ‥‥弁護人。
English: Hmmmm... Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: その人物‥‥本当に、あの晩 スノーモービルを見ているのかな？
English: This individual... Did they really see the snowmobile that night?

Japanese: どなたですかな？　この、決定的に キンチョー感を欠いた青年は。
English: This guy must be a product of "Jean-Luc de Laduc's Guide To Obnoxious French Painting"...

Japanese: 矢張　政志。‥‥被害者、 天流斎　エリスの弟子、だ。
English: This is Larry Butz... The student of the victim, Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: 被害者の‥‥？ それは、おもしろい。
English: Her student...? Interesting.

Japanese: 彼は、なぜ‥‥事件当夜、オモテを ほっつき歩いていたのですか？
English: Why was he wandering on the night of the murder?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: T-That's...

Japanese: （あやめさんのことをブチまける のは、たやすいが‥‥）
English: (I could tell them all about his designs for Iris...)

Japanese: （証人としての信頼を、呼び出す 前から失うおそれがある‥‥）
English: (But it may cost us his credibility as a witness... Before I even call him.)

Japanese: まあ‥‥彼もまた、芸術家の はしくれだ。
English: He is, after all, an artist.

Japanese: 雪景色に、何かココロ打たれる ものを感じたのではないだろうか。
English: He was, perhaps, searching for something in the snowy scenery that would move him.

Japanese: ‥‥保証はしないが。
English: ...Although I cannot guarantee that this is the reason.

Japanese: それで‥‥この、 たよりなさそうなオトコが‥‥
English: And so...? This unfortunate, unreliable-looking man...

Japanese: いったい、ナニを 見たというのかしら？
English: What exactly was it that he saw?

Japanese: それを、この場で 聞き出そうと言っている。
English: I intend to extract that from him, right here in this courtroom.

Japanese: ‥‥大至急、その青年を 証人として召喚しましょう！
English: Summon this youth as a witness immediately!

Japanese: やむを得ないようね。
English: I have no choice, do I?

Japanese: 彼はおそらく、この法廷を 傍聴しているはず。
English: I believe he is in the gallery for this trial.

Japanese: 召喚に、時間はかかるまい。
English: It will not take long to summon him.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...Very well.
(Larry...)

Japanese: （きのうは、うまく 逃げられてしまったが‥‥）
English: (You may have escaped me yesterday...)

Japanese: （今度こそ、すべてを 引きずり出してやるぞ！）
English: (But today I'm going to get everything out of you!)

Japanese: ‥‥これより、２０分間の 休憩をとりたいと思います。
English: ...The court will now adjourn for a 20 minute break.

Japanese: 検察側は、証人の手配を おねがいします。
English: Ms. von Karma, please see to preparing the next witness.

Japanese: ‥‥了解したわ。
English: ...Understood, Your Honor.

Japanese: よろしい‥‥。
English: Good...

Japanese: それでは！ 休憩に入ります！
English: Well then! Court is now in recess!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、葉桜院　あやめに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Iris of Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
